code: pr_full   <- make_ferralsol_canonical() pr_partial <- pr_full$clone(deep = TRUE) pr_partial$horizons[3:5, c("cec_cmol", "bs_pct") := NA]  pr_partial$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, designation,                         clay_pct, cec_cmol, bs_pct, oc_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm designation clay_pct cec_cmol bs_pct oc_pct #>     <num>     <num>      <char>    <num>    <num>  <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15           A       50      8.0     24    2.0 #> 2:     15        35          AB       52      6.5     17    1.2 #> 3:     35        65          BA       55       NA     NA    0.6 #> 4:     65       130         Bw1       60       NA     NA    0.3 #> 5:    130       200         Bw2       60       NA     NA    0.2 res_partial <- classify_wrb2022(pr_partial, on_missing = "silent") res_partial$rsg_or_order #> [1] "Nitisols" res_partial$evidence_grade #> [1] "A"

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: prior probability vector RSGs source – regional SoilGrids extract, national Embrapa map, interpolated kriging surface, etc. vignette build inline example runs without network access: prior_consistency_check() confirms deterministic call (FR) supported prior: Now suppose deterministic key instead landed Cambisols. prior flag disagreement (Cambisols probability 0.12 vs dominant Ferralsols 0.62 – inconsistency margin): deterministic key never overwritten prior. check flags cases manual review warranted; user remains charge final assignment. Real production runs source prior spatial_prior_soilgrids() (live SoilGrids-WCS request) spatial_prior_embrapa():
code: # Synthetic prior consistent with the gneiss-Mata-Atlantica context: # Ferralsols dominate, with a tail of Acrisols and Cambisols. prior <- data.table::data.table(   rsg_code    = c("FR", "AC", "CM", "AL"),   probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.12, 0.06) ) prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 #> 4:       AL        0.06 chk <- prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "FR", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) chk #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.62 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'FR' has prior probability 0.620 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior_consistency_check(rsg_code = "AL", prior = prior, threshold = 0.05) #> $consistent #> [1] TRUE #>  #> $p #> [1] 0.06 #>  #> $threshold #> [1] 0.05 #>  #> $status #> [1] "consistent" #>  #> $note #> [1] "Assigned RSG 'AL' has prior probability 0.060 at this location (>= threshold 0.050)." #>  #> $top_prior #>    rsg_code probability #>      <char>       <num> #> 1:       FR        0.62 #> 2:       AC        0.20 #> 3:       CM        0.12 prior <- spatial_prior_soilgrids(pr_partial, buffer_m = 250)

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: OSSL workflow : Pre-process raw spectra (SNV / Savitzky-Golay 1st derivative). predict_ossl_mbl() – memory-based learning (recommended); predict_ossl_plsr_local() – partial-least-squares local subset; predict_ossl_pretrained() – pre-trained Cubist RF model. Convert property’s prediction-interval width –D confidence grade via pi_to_confidence(). fill_from_spectra() writes predicted value horizons table adds provenance entry source = "predicted_spectra". production call look like (skipped vignette OSSL multi-GB dataset downloaded): vignette, simulate predicted values directly pedon$add_measurement(): predicted values now horizons table, provenance log records predicted_spectra:
code: fill_from_spectra(   pr_partial,   library     = "ossl",   region      = "south_america",   properties  = c("clay_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "oc_pct"),   method      = "mbl",   preprocess  = "snv+sg1",   k_neighbors = 100L,   ossl_library = "/path/to/ossl-soilsite-vnir.parquet" ) preds <- list(   list(idx = 3, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 5.5, confidence = 0.78),   list(idx = 3, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 14,  confidence = 0.72),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.9, confidence = 0.79),   list(idx = 4, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.74),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "cec_cmol", value = 4.7, confidence = 0.70),   list(idx = 5, attribute = "bs_pct",   value = 13,  confidence = 0.71) )  pr_filled <- pr_partial$clone(deep = TRUE) for (p in preds) {   pr_filled$add_measurement(     horizon_idx = p$idx,     attribute   = p$attribute,     value       = p$value,     source      = "predicted_spectra",     confidence  = p$confidence,     overwrite   = TRUE   ) }  pr_filled$horizons[, .(top_cm, bottom_cm, cec_cmol, bs_pct)] #>    top_cm bottom_cm cec_cmol bs_pct #>     <num>     <num>    <num>  <num> #> 1:      0        15      8.0     24 #> 2:     15        35      6.5     17 #> 3:     35        65      5.5     14 #> 4:     65       130      4.9     13 #> 5:    130       200      4.7     13 prov <- pr_filled$provenance prov[source == "predicted_spectra", .(horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence)] #>    horizon_idx attribute            source confidence #>          <int>    <char>            <char>      <num> #> 1:           3  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.78 #> 2:           3    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.72 #> 3:           4  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.79 #> 4:           4    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.74 #> 5:           5  cec_cmol predicted_spectra       0.70 #> 6:           5    bs_pct predicted_spectra       0.71

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: combine_priors() merges multiple sources (SoilGrids + Embrapa + custom map) weights, returning single normalised prior can feed prior_consistency_check(): posterior_classify() take ClassificationResult prior, returning posterior probability RSGs (deterministic key contributes sharply peaked likelihood). Used ranking ambiguous fixtures active-learning loops.
code: combined <- combine_priors(   priors = list(     soilgrids = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "CM"),                                           probability = c(0.62, 0.20, 0.18)),     embrapa   = data.table::data.table(rsg_code = c("FR", "AC", "NT"),                                           probability = c(0.55, 0.30, 0.15))   ),   weights = c(soilgrids = 0.6, embrapa = 0.4) ) combined #>    rsg_code  probability #>      <char>        <num> #> 1:       FR 0.7139748011 #> 2:       AC 0.2841641547 #> 3:       CM 0.0017189621 #> 4:       NT 0.0001420821

title: Spatial prior + OSSL spectra pipeline (Modules 3 & 4)
text: deterministic key always runs first never overwritten. Module 3 gives probabilistic sanity check external maps warns disagreement. Module 4 fills missing horizon attributes spectra, full provenance, evidence grade tracks substitution. Putting together, can take partially-described pedon, fill gaps spectra, classify deterministically, cross-check result global map – one pipeline. next vignette (v06_wosis_benchmark) shows run whole stack scale WoSIS global pedon archive paper-grade validation.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: benchmark profile triple (P,T,C)(P, T, C): PP – PedonRecord assembled source dataset’s horizons + chemistry; TT – target RSG name source’s classification (e.g. WoSIS’ cwrb_reference_soil_group); CC – candidate classification produced classify_wrb2022(P). benchmark reports four numbers per source: Top-1 agreement – fraction profiles C$rsg=TC\$rsg = T. Top-3 agreement – fraction TT among three highest-likelihood RSGs trace (key trace + spatial prior, available). Coverage – fraction profiles CC definite RSG (“Regosols” catch-). Indeterminate-rate – fraction profiles C$rsgC\$rsg NA owing missing diagnostic attributes. Confusion matrices reported alongside headline numbers: RSGs soilKey confuses (e.g. Acrisol vs Lixisol differ base saturation, disagreement border informative).

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: Beyond RSG, classify_wrb2022() returns principal- supplementary-qualifier lists. benchmark can deepened track agreement qualifier prefix soil name (strongest principal fired). canonical fixtures: -specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture.
code: qualifier_prefix <- vapply(names(expected), function(code) {   fx <- fixfns[[code]]()   res <- classify_wrb2022(fx, on_missing = "silent")   if (length(res$qualifiers$principal) == 0) NA_character_   else res$qualifiers$principal[1] }, character(1))  knitr::kable(   data.frame(fixture          = names(qualifier_prefix),              principal_prefix = qualifier_prefix),   caption = "Most-specific principal qualifier per canonical fixture." )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: mini-benchmark proves package’s internal consistency. paper-grade benchmark runs flow ISRIC WoSIS, global archive ~118 000 georeferenced pedon descriptions WRB 2014 / 2022 classifications. protocol intentionally separated package (live download vignettes) can re-run controlled date. driver script shipped inst/benchmarks/run_wosis_benchmark.R. Skeleton: flow extends naturally : WoSIS subsets region – e.g. South America , Brazilian profiles (relevant SiBCS cross-system check). WoSIS profiles missing one attributes – exercise gap-filling pipeline (vignette 05) report agreement uplift predicted_spectra. WoSIS profiles labelled v2014 vs v2022 RSGs – benchmark separates soilKey’s v2022-key penalised WoSIS used deprecated RSG name.
code: library(soilKey)  # 1. Pull a snapshot of WoSIS profiles via the WoSIS API. profiles <- read_wosis_profiles(   url     = "https://wosis.isric.org/api/v3/profiles?format=json",   page_size = 500L )  # 2. Build a PedonRecord per profile. pedons <- lapply(profiles, build_pedon_from_wosis)  # 3. Classify each through the v0.9.3 key. classifications <- lapply(pedons, classify_wrb2022, on_missing = "silent")  # 4. Compare against the WoSIS-recorded RSG. bench <- mapply(function(c, p) {   data.frame(     profile_id  = p$site$id,     target_rsg  = p$site$wosis_rsg,     assigned    = c$rsg_or_order,     grade       = c$evidence_grade,     match       = c$rsg_or_order == p$site$wosis_rsg   ) }, classifications, pedons, SIMPLIFY = FALSE) bench <- do.call(rbind, bench)  # 5. Headline numbers. list(   n              = nrow(bench),   top1           = mean(bench$match, na.rm = TRUE),   indeterminate  = mean(is.na(bench$assigned)),   pct_grade_A    = mean(bench$grade == "A"),   by_rsg         = table(bench$target_rsg, bench$assigned) )

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: benchmark run produces versioned report inst/benchmarks/reports/wosis_<DATE>.md containing: Snapshot date WoSIS subset used. Top-1 / Top-3 agreement, 95% CI non-parametric bootstrap. Confusion matrix (RSG-level) qualifier-prefix agreement. Per-RSG agreement common confused pair . Evidence-grade distribution. Notable disagreements diagnostic-level traces, QA. script used periodic regression run ships minor soilKey release.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: package ships offline benchmark driver alongside WoSIS one — run_canonical_benchmark() inst/benchmarks/run_wosis_benchmark.R — loops 31 canonical fixtures inst/extdata/, runs three keys, compares known target encoded filename cross-system correspondence table (Schad 2023 Annex Table 1; SiBCS 5ª ed. Annex ), writes versioned report inst/benchmarks/reports/canonical_<DATE>.md. Sourcing file running package root produces real, reproducible numbers without network call: recent canonical run (committed inst/benchmarks/reports/) achieved: canonical run release-time CI check key + qualifier system + cross-system mapping hold ; WoSIS run paper-grade external validation.
code: source(system.file("benchmarks", "run_wosis_benchmark.R",                     package = "soilKey")) bench <- run_canonical_benchmark()

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: soilKey’s benchmarking layer intentionally minimal: mini-benchmark canonical fixtures (vignette) fully reproducible inside rendered HTML acts CI check key + qualifier system holds 100% top-1 agreement profiles constructed match RSG. offline run_canonical_benchmark() driver runs three systems multi-valued cross-system targets writes versioned report — invoked per release, network-free. full WoSIS benchmark run--per-release, -line paper, driver script reports versioned inst/benchmarks/. three layers report metrics comparison across releases direct. closes methodological loop opened architecture document: deterministic key, side modules (VLM, spatial, OSSL), benchmark integrated without able overrule others.

title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: ISRIC WoSIS GraphQL endpoint occasionally returns “canceling statement due statement timeout” load — observed consistently ~40 profiles per session May 2026. naïve read_wosis_profiles_graphql() abort whole pull surface hard error; unfriendly 30+ profiles already came back fine. v0.9.27 adds per-page retry exponential backoff (1 s, 2 s, 4 s, 8 s; 4 attempts) plus graceful degradation: least one page succeeded, transient failures later pages return partial pull rather aborting. makes live WoSIS benchmark robust enough run CI casual user sessions without surprise crashes.
code: source(system.file("benchmarks", "run_wosis_benchmark.R",                     package = "soilKey")) profs <- read_wosis_profiles_graphql(   continent = "South America",   n_max     = 100L,   page_size = 10L,   verbose   = TRUE ) length(profs) #> [1] 40   # 100 was requested but server timed out at offset = 40; #>          # the partial pull was returned cleanly.

what: 8. v0.9.30 – bundled WoSIS sample for offline / CI testing
title: Benchmarking soilKey against WoSIS
text: tests, CI, casual users want exercise WRB benchmark path without depending ISRIC server availability, v0.9.30 ships frozen 40-profile snapshot inst/extdata/wosis_sa_sample.rds (49 KB). Helper: bundled snapshot reproducible tests, current ground-truth claims. --date paper-grade benchmarks, callers still use run_wosis_benchmark_graphql() live endpoint. multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark (Order / Suborder / Great Group / Subgroup) KSSL gpkg + NASIS sqlite join, see vignette v08_kssl_nasis_multilevel.
code: sample <- load_wosis_sample() length(sample$pedons) #> [1] 40  sample$pulled_on #> [1] "2026-05-03"  # Classify offline: classify_wrb2022(sample$pedons[[1]])$rsg_or_order

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: use canonical Latossolo Vermelho Distrocoeso Mata Atlântica around Seropédica, RJ, parent material gneiss. fixture mimics real Embrapa survey profile.
code: # Field GPS coordinates of the planned profile pit. field_lat <- -22.7 field_lon <- -43.7

what: 2. Spatial guide – before any pedon data
title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soil_classes_at_location() queries SoilGrids 2.0 (WRB-coded raster user provides) returns ranked list likely classes plus canonical attribute thresholds distinguish . function classify – tells pedologist “likely standing Ferralsol; look confirm”.
code: guide <- soil_classes_at_location(   lat        = field_lat,   lon        = field_lon,   system     = "wrb2022",   source_url = "https://files.isric.org/soilgrids/latest/data/wrb/MostProbable.vrt" )  guide$distribution #> # Ranked candidate classes: #> # rsg_code  rsg_name      probability #> # FR        Ferralsols    0.62 #> # AC        Acrisols      0.21 #> # NT        Nitisols      0.12 #> # CM        Cambisols     0.05 guide$typical_attributes #> # Per-class diagnostic thresholds to confirm in the field.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: pedologist arrives pit, photographs wall Munsell chart, scans field sheet, exports survey report PDF. classify_from_documents() chains entire downstream pipeline – VLM extraction, three classifications, optional report rendering – single call. default provider local Gemma 4 edge (gemma4:e4b, ~3 GB, multimodal text + image + audio) via Ollama – API key, data leaving laptop. Pull model : Every extracted attribute stamped source = "extracted_vlm" PedonRecord’s provenance log; deterministic key consumed PedonRecord unaware value got . architectural invariant – key never delegated model – holds. rest vignette keep working populated pedon res$pedon.
code: ollama pull gemma4:e4b ollama serve res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html",   provider = "ollama"  # default; uses gemma4:e4b )  res$classifications$wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)" res$classifications$sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" res$classifications$usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox" # For a runnable demo without Ollama / a real PDF, reuse the # canonical Ferralsol fixture -- the downstream code is the same. pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: Vis-NIR scan available surface horizon, classify_by_spectral_neighbours() adds another evidence layer. finds K spectrally similar OSSL profiles within regional radius returns probabilistic class prediction. biome-aware regional filter prevents analogy drifting non-tropical reference soils.
code: # Hypothetical: a real OSSL South-America library with WRB labels # obtained via `download_ossl_subset_with_labels()`. ossl_lib <- download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region          = "south_america",   max_distance_km = 10 )  # Pull the surface-horizon Vis-NIR scan from the populated pedon. query_spectrum <- pedon$spectra$vnir[1, ]  spectral <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = query_spectrum,   ossl_library = ossl_lib,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = field_lat, lon = field_lon,                       radius_km = 500) ) spectral$distribution #> # class    n_neighbours  probability #> # FR              22       0.88 #> # AC               2       0.08 #> # NT               1       0.04 spectral$neighbours #> # The 25 closest OSSL profiles + their distances + labels.

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: canonical step. classify_wrb2022() / classify_sibcs() / classify_usda() walk canonical YAML rules populated PedonRecord.
code: cls_wrb   <- classify_wrb2022(pedon, on_missing = "silent") cls_sibcs <- classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE) cls_usda  <- classify_usda(pedon)  cls_wrb$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Eutric, Ochric, Rubic)" cls_sibcs$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado" cls_usda$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox"  # Each ClassificationResult carries the full key trace, the per- # attribute provenance, and an evidence grade A/B/C/D. cls_wrb$evidence_grade #> [1] "A" length(cls_wrb$trace)         # number of RSGs tested before assignment #> [1] 16

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report() writes self-contained HTML one-pager cross-system summary, full key trace, evidence grade, qualifiers, ambiguities, missing-data hints, horizons table, per-source provenance summary. output single HTML file inline CSS – external network requests, suitable emailing colleague attaching laudo.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report(results, file = "perfil_042.html", pedon = pedon)

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: report_to_qgis() produces multi-layer GeoPackage (.gpkg) QGIS reads natively. GeoPackage carries three layers: pedon_point – POINT geometry profile coordinates classification metadata attributes (WRB / SiBCS / USDA names, RSG / Ordem / Order codes, evidence grades, principal qualifiers, supplementary qualifiers, hyperlink rendered HTML report). horizons_table – one row per horizon, canonical horizon-schema attributes. Joined pedon_point site_id. provenance_log – per-(horizon, attribute, source) provenance rows downstream auditing. QGIS: Layer → Add Layer → Add Vector Layer → perfil_042.gpkg. point appears canvas classification metadata feature pop-; styling rules can map symbol colour evidence grade assigned RSG.
code: results <- list(wrb = cls_wrb, sibcs = cls_sibcs, usda = cls_usda) report_to_qgis(   pedon           = pedon,   classifications = results,   file            = "perfil_042.gpkg",   report_html     = "perfil_042.html" )

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: step’s output carries explicit provenance next; final evidence_grade reflects worst-source rule applied attributes decisive assigned name. Two pedologists running pipeline documents get output bit--bit.
code: # Pipeline summary: # #   field GPS      ->  soil_classes_at_location()         "what to expect" #                                  | #                                  v #   PDF + photo    ->  classify_from_documents() (Gemma 4)  populates PedonRecord #                                  | #                                  v #   Vis-NIR scan   ->  classify_by_spectral_neighbours()    spectral prior #                                  | #                                  v #                  ->  classify_wrb2022()  + classify_sibcs() + classify_usda() #                                  |       (the deterministic step -- canonical) #                                  v #                  ->  report() / report_to_qgis()         deliverables

title: End-to-end pipeline: Gemma 4 + spatial + spectral + key + GIS export
text: soilKey separates four distinct stages: Spatial guides (soil_classes_at_location) – expectations soil-class raster. Extraction (classify_from_documents, extract_*) – VLM populates PedonRecord, never classifies. Spectral analogy (classify_by_spectral_neighbours) – OSSL nearest-neighbour analogy prior. Deterministic classification (classify_wrb2022 / classify_sibcs / classify_usda) – canonical step. Plus two delivery formats: HTML reports (report) GeoPackage exports (report_to_qgis). four stages preserve provenance evidence grading; deterministic key remains thing assigns class.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: Kellogg Soil Survey Laboratory (NCSS lab data, USDA-NRCS — NCSS Lab Data Mart, ncsslabdatamart.sc.egov.usda.gov) ships ~36 000 pedons full analytical chemistry (clay, sand, silt, OC, pH, CEC, BS, …) minimal field morphology. NASIS Morphological database (separate sqlite distribution) carries matching field-survey morphology: Munsell colors, structure grade / size / type, clay films (phpvsf), slickensides, pediagfeatures diagnostic-features table (~13 500 entries identifying argillic, mollic, cambic, spodic, fragipan, etc.). load_kssl_pedons_with_nasis() joins two peiid depth-overlap matching, returning one PedonRecord per profile lab morphology populated: NASIS-derived attributes coverage 2021 snapshot (n=865 quality-filtered): high Munsell + structure coverage powers pediagfeatures tie-breaker (v0.9.21) – canonical lab + morphology gate returns passed = NA, surveyor’s diagnostic flag flips TRUE. argillic / fragipan / clay-films paths (v0.9.27, v0.9.28, v0.9.31) leverage NASIS slot direct positive evidence.
code: peds <- load_kssl_pedons_with_nasis(   gpkg   = "<path>/ncss_labdata.gpkg",   sqlite = "<path>/NASIS_Morphological_09142021.sqlite",   head   = 3000)  length(peds) #> [1] 2964   # after the 3000-row gpkg slice and the join  peds[[1]]$horizons[, c("designation", "clay_pct", "munsell_chroma_moist",                           "clay_films_amount")] #>    designation clay_pct munsell_chroma_moist clay_films_amount #> 1: Ap          22.5     3                    NA #> 2: Bt1         34.7     4                    common #> 3: Bt2         38.3     4                    many

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: list PedonRecords reference_usda, reference_usda_subgroup, reference_usda_grtgroup, reference_usda_suborder populated (gpkg loader handles four), benchmark_run_classification() measures top-1 accuracy four hierarchy levels:
code: # Quality filter: profiles with usable clay data and a non-empty # subgroup reference label. keep <- vapply(peds, function(p) {   hz <- p$horizons   if (is.null(hz) || nrow(hz) == 0) return(FALSE)   if (!any(!is.na(hz$clay_pct))) return(FALSE)   !is.null(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) &&     !is.na(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) &&     nzchar(p$site$reference_usda_subgroup) }, logical(1)) peds <- peds[keep]  for (lvl in c("order", "suborder", "great_group", "subgroup")) {   res <- benchmark_run_classification(peds, system = "usda",                                          level = lvl, boot_n = 500L)   cat(sprintf("%-12s n=%d  top1=%.4f  CI=[%.3f, %.3f]\n",                 lvl, res$n_evaluated, res$accuracy_top1,                 res$accuracy_ci[1], res$accuracy_ci[2])) }

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: values reported methodology paper. measured n=2638 large-scale validation sample (3x n=865 development sample) 500 bootstrap reps ±1.7 pp confidence intervals.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: v0.9.24 → v0.9.31 release series progressively closed key reasoning gaps. Apples--apples n=865 development sample: v0.9.25 KST 13ed Great Group canonicaliser remains largest single-version GG lift (+3.84 pp, +59 % relative). required classifier changes — predictor already correct KST 13ed; comparison unfair legacy KSSL labels (span Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13).

what: 5. The four levels are independently measurable
title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: level = "great_group" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_grtgroup compares LAST token classify_usda(pedon)$name v0.9.25 KST canonicaliser collapses edition-driven renames. level = "suborder" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_suborder applies .gg_to_suborder() (~70-Suborder canonical-suffix mapping derived KST 13ed Ch 4) predicted Great Group name. level = "subgroup" reads pedon$site$reference_usda_subgroup compares full subgroup name token--token (Subgroup modifier kept intact; Great Group canonicalised). level = "order" reads pedon$site$reference_usda compares top-level Order name directly.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: KSSL samp_taxgrtgroup populated historical pedon descriptions spanning Soil Taxonomy editions 8 13. Several Great Group names changed editions, KSSL retroactively update . soilKey’s classifier follows KST 13ed (current edition), direct string equality predicted (13ed) reference (mixed editions) Great Group names produces false-negative misses every profile whose KSSL label pre-13ed name. 16 common edition-driven renames KSSL: canonicaliser canonicalise_kst13ed_gg() collapses pair (triple, Hapl-/Endo-/Epi- splits) shared canonical key. Apply ref pred comparing – Subgroup modifier (Typic / Aquic / …) left intact canonicalisation affects Great Group token.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: 4.2 % Subgroup top-1 leaves substantial headroom. v0.9.27 confusion analysis identified dominant remaining gaps: Pale- / Glossic Alfisol prefixes (~11 misses, deferred v0.9.32+): current pale_qualifying_usda() proxy strict (clay >= 35 %); KST 13ed actually defines Pale- clay-stability within 150 cm. Tightening without regressing Hapludalfs delicate work. NASIS data sparsity (~47 % profiles lack pediagfeatures / phpvsf record): roadmap candidate field-survey morphology + soilKey’s pedometric proxies third evidence path. Endo/Epi-aquic precise distinction: currently v0.9.25 canonicaliser collapses fair comparison; future release restore distinction inferring saturation depth redoximorphic_features_pct distribution.

title: KSSL + NASIS: multi-level USDA Soil Taxonomy benchmark
text: KSSL + NASIS join soilKey’s headline real-data benchmark. exercises every level USDA Soil Taxonomy 13ed hierarchy n=2638 profiles full lab + morphology data, confidence intervals tight enough (±1.7 pp) paper-grade claims. v0.9.24 → v0.9.31 release series moved Great Group accuracy 6.5 % baseline 10.92 %, primarily KST 13ed name canonicaliser (v0.9.25) collapsed edition-driven renames historical KSSL labels. WRB SiBCS benchmark axes, see vignettes v06_wosis_benchmark FEBR / Embrapa report inst/benchmarks/reports/embrapa_v0929_2026-05-03.md.

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esta vinheta acompanha um unico perfil de solo Brasil tropical desde os dados brutos de campo + laboratorio ate tres classificacoes paralelas (SiBCS 5a edicao, WRB 2022, USDA Soil Taxonomy 13a edicao), passando por: construcao PedonRecord formato canonico soilKey; inspecao dos diagnosticos disparados (B textural, atividade da argila, V%); classificacao tripla com explicacao trace; relatorio HTML pronto para entregar um colega ou aluno; (opcional) cruzamento com o priore espacial MapBiomas Solos v0.9.48. O objetivo e dar ao agronomo / pedologo brasileiro um caminho reproduzivel e em portugues, estilo dos boletins da Embrapa Solos. Todo o codigo abaixo roda sem dependencia externa pesada (nem rede, nem ESDB, nem OSSL); etapa final MapBiomas e opcional e exige um raster baixado.

what: O perfil: Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo distrofico tipico
title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Adaptado Levantamento Reconhecimento dos Solos Estado Rio de Janeiro (Embrapa, 2003), perfil RJ-1 da regiao de Itaguai. Coordenadas aproximadas: 22.86 S, 43.78 W. Material de origem: sedimentos argilosos Terciario. Construindo o PedonRecord (R6 com site + horizons):
code: horizontes <- data.table::data.table(   top_cm                = c(0,    20,   55,   115,  170),   bottom_cm             = c(20,   55,   115,  170,  220),   designation           = c("A",  "AB", "Bt1","Bt2","BC"),   munsell_hue_moist     = c("10YR","7.5YR","5YR","2.5YR","2.5YR"),   munsell_value_moist   = c(4,     4,     4,     3,     3),   munsell_chroma_moist  = c(3,     5,     6,     6,     6),   structure_grade       = c("moderate", "moderate",                               "strong", "strong", "moderate"),   structure_type        = c("granular", "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky",                               "subangular blocky"),   clay_films_amount     = c(NA, "few", "common", "common", "few"),   clay_pct              = c(18,   28,   45,   42,   38),   silt_pct              = c(30,   25,   20,   22,   24),   sand_pct              = c(52,   47,   35,   36,   38),   ph_h2o                = c(5.5,  5.3,  5.0,  5.0,  5.1),   ph_kcl                = c(4.4,  4.3,  4.1,  4.1,  4.2),   oc_pct                = c(1.5,  0.6,  0.3,  0.2,  0.2),   cec_cmol              = c(8.0,  6.0,  5.5,  4.5,  4.0),   bs_pct                = c(35,   25,   20,   18,   20),   # V baixa -> distrofico   al_cmol               = c(0.5,  0.8,  1.2,  1.5,  1.4),   ca_cmol               = c(2.0,  1.4,  1.0,  0.7,  0.7),   mg_cmol               = c(0.6,  0.4,  0.3,  0.2,  0.2),   k_cmol                = c(0.10, 0.06, 0.04, 0.03, 0.03),   na_cmol               = c(0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02, 0.02),   bulk_density_g_cm3    = c(1.30, 1.40, 1.45, 1.45, 1.42) ) horizontes <- soilKey:::ensure_horizon_schema(horizontes) str(horizontes[, .(designation, top_cm, bottom_cm, ph_h2o, clay_pct, bs_pct)]) #> Classes 'data.table' and 'data.frame':   5 obs. of  6 variables: #>  $ designation: chr  "A" "AB" "Bt1" "Bt2" ... #>  $ top_cm     : num  0 20 55 115 170 #>  $ bottom_cm  : num  20 55 115 170 220 #>  $ ph_h2o     : num  5.5 5.3 5 5 5.1 #>  $ clay_pct   : num  18 28 45 42 38 #>  $ bs_pct     : num  35 25 20 18 20 #>  - attr(*, ".internal.selfref")=<pointer: 0x5615a0d96f00> perfil <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(     id        = "RJ-1-Itaguai",     lat       = -22.86,     lon       = -43.78,     country   = "BR",     state     = "RJ",     municipality = "Itaguai",     parent_material = "sedimentos argilosos do Terciario",     survey_year = 2003,     reference_source = "Embrapa Solos (2003) - Levantamento RJ"   ),   horizons = horizontes ) perfil #>  #> ── PedonRecord ── #>  #> Site: id=RJ-1-Itaguai | (-22.8600, -43.7800) | BR | on sedimentos argilosos do #> Terciario #> Horizons (5): #> 1) A 0-20 cm clay=18.0 silt=30.0 sand=52.0 CEC=8.0 pH=5.5 OC=1.5 #> 2) AB 20-55 cm clay=28.0 silt=25.0 sand=47.0 CEC=6.0 pH=5.3 OC=0.6 #> 3) Bt1 55-115 cm clay=45.0 silt=20.0 sand=35.0 CEC=5.5 pH=5.0 OC=0.3 #> 4) Bt2 115-170 cm clay=42.0 silt=22.0 sand=36.0 CEC=4.5 pH=5.0 OC=0.2 #> 5) BC 170-220 cm clay=38.0 silt=24.0 sand=38.0 CEC=4.0 pH=5.1 OC=0.2

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Antes de chamar chave completa, vale rodar os predicados-chave para sentir o perfil. Isso e equivalente ao trabalho classificador humano antes de aplicar chave: Os tres devem disparar TRUE para um Argissolo distrofico tipico.
code:
# B textural (SiBCS Cap 5 / WRB argic): gradiente de argila bt   <- soilKey::B_textural(perfil) arg  <- soilKey::argic(perfil) cat("B_textural (SiBCS):", bt$passed,     "  argic (WRB):",     arg$passed, "
") #> B_textural (SiBCS): TRUE   argic (WRB): TRUE  # Atividade da argila (SiBCS Cap 5) ta <- soilKey:::atividade_argila_alta(perfil) cat("atividade_argila_alta:", ta$passed, "
") #> atividade_argila_alta: FALSE  # Saturacao por bases (V%) -- distrofico se V < 50 distr <- soilKey::distrofico(perfil) cat("distrofico:", distr$passed, "
") #> distrofico: TRUE

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: funcao classify_all() corre os tres sistemas e devolve um ClassificationResult por sistema:
code: res <- soilKey::classify_all(perfil, on_missing = "silent") names(res) #> [1] "wrb"     "sibcs"   "usda"    "summary"

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: descida vai nivel da Ordem (Argissolos) ate o Subgrupo, passando pelos discriminantes: Matiz Munsell em B (5YR / 2.5YR) -> Subordem Vermelho-Amarelo V% baixa em B -> Grande Grupo Distrofico Atividade da argila baixa, sem cerosidade forte -> Subgrupo tipico

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: WRB devolve o RSG mais qualificadores (em ordem canonica): Acrisol Dystric Cutanic (caracter argico + V baixa + presenca de revestimentos argilosos).

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esses tres rotulos sao complementares, nao competidores. O SiBCS captura nuance brasileira (Distrofico, Tb, presenca/ausencia de cerosidade); o WRB captura o consenso internacional (Acrisol); o USDA-ST encaixa esquema norte-americano (Ultisol). Todos os tres apontam para mesma realidade: um solo argico, acido, baixa em bases, vermelho-amarelo.
code: data.frame(   Sistema  = c("SiBCS 5a", "WRB 2022", "USDA-ST 13a"),   Classe   = c(res$sibcs$name, res$wrb$name, res$usda$name),   EvidGrade = c(res$sibcs$evidence_grade %||% NA,                   res$wrb$evidence_grade   %||% NA,                   res$usda$evidence_grade  %||% NA) ) #>       Sistema #> 1    SiBCS 5a #> 2    WRB 2022 #> 3 USDA-ST 13a #>                                                                                Classe #> 1                  Argissolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado, Tmob #> 2 Ferralic Cutanic Acrisol (Clayic, Loamic, Eutric, Ochric, Profondic, Rhodic, Rubic) #> 3                                                                  Rhodic Kandiudults #>   EvidGrade #> 1         A #> 2         A #> 3         A

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: O HTML carrega: nome canonico em cada sistema, qualificadores, evidencia grade, trace dos predicados, ambiguidades, atributos faltantes, e os horizontes formatados estilo planilha de boletim.
code: res$sibcs$report(   file  = "perfil_RJ1.html",   format = "html",   pedon = perfil ) # Ou, para os tres sistemas em um arquivo: soilKey::report(   list(res$sibcs, res$wrb, res$usda),   file  = "perfil_RJ1_triplo.html",   format = "html",   pedon = perfil )

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Se voce baixou o raster nacional MapBiomas Solos Colecao 2 (30 m, classes SiBCS, 2023+), pode cruzar coordenada perfil com o mapa para verificar consistencia: Concordancia entre o classificador e o mapa nacional aumenta confianca. Discordancia (ex.: classificador diz Argissolo, mapa diz Latossolo) e um sinal de revisao – o pedologo decide qual fonte e mais confiavel para o caso.
code: sibcs_no_mapa <- soilKey::lookup_mapbiomas_solos(   coords      = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   raster_path = "soil_data/mapbiomas/mapbiomas_solos_30m_2023.tif",   legend      = data.frame(     value      = c(3),     class_name = c("Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo")   ) ) sibcs_no_mapa #> [1] "Argissolo Vermelho-Amarelo"

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Para qualquer coordenada mundo, lookup_soilgrids() (v0.9.48) le direto endpoint COG da ISRIC sem download:
code: ph_topsoil <- soilKey::lookup_soilgrids(   coords   = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   property = "phh2o",  depth = "0-5cm", quantile = "mean" ) clay_subsoil <- soilKey::lookup_soilgrids(   coords   = c(perfil$site$lon, perfil$site$lat),   property = "clay",   depth = "30-60cm", quantile = "mean" ) cat("SoilGrids pH (0-5cm):", ph_topsoil,     " | clay subsoil (30-60cm):", clay_subsoil, "%\n")

title: Um perfil real do A ao Z (estilo Embrapa, em portugues)
text: Esse e o caminho reproduzivel que o pacote oferece: o pedologo chega com horizontes + cores Munsell + analises de laboratorio, roda um comando (classify_all()), e recebe os tres rotulos junto com o trace explicando exatamente quais diagnosticos dispararam em cada sistema. Em ~10 linhas de R.

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: pro interface needs optional packages bslib, shinyWidgets plotly addition shiny DT. missing, run_classify_app() stops copy-pasteable install.packages() line. classic interface needs shiny DT.
code: library(soilKey)  # The professional multi-tab app (default). run_classify_app()  # Equivalent, explicit: run_classify_app(ui = "pro")  # The original single-page CSV uploader (v0.9.39 layout): run_classify_app(ui = "classic") install.packages(c("shiny", "DT", "bslib", "shinyWidgets", "plotly"))

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Seed profile three ways: Fixture – pick one 44 canonical profiles (31 WRB reference soil groups + 13 SiBCS ordens). fixtures test suite uses, guaranteed classify cleanly. Upload CSV – one row per horizon. Columns matched canonical horizon schema; unknown columns kept, missing ones filled NA. starter CSV available sidebar. Blank – start empty template build profile hand. Every horizon cell editable place – click cell, type, press Enter. depth-profile plot right updates live; switch plotted attribute dropdown. table looks right, fill site metadata (ID, latitude, longitude, country, parent material) press Build / update pedon.

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Runs WRB 2022, SiBCS 5 USDA Soil Taxonomy 13 side--side. result card shows full classification name, bare RSG / order, principal supplementary qualifiers, provenance-aware evidence grade. Decision detail panel exposes: Key trace – every RSG tested, key order, pass / fail / indeterminate status. Failed passed rows colour-coded. Ambiguities – RSGs nearly satisfied; useful hints follow-measurement. Missing data – attributes whose measurement refine resolve result.

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Drives vision-language extraction pipeline. profile photograph yields Munsell colour per horizon; field-sheet image yields site metadata. taxonomic key never delegated model – extraction fills PedonRecord. default Demo (mock) provider returns canned, schema-valid response pipeline runs offline API key. use live model, configure ellmer chat object launching:
code: options(soilKey.vlm_chat = ellmer::chat_anthropic()) run_classify_app()

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Attach Vis-NIR spectrum (one row per horizon, one column per wavelength) gap-fill missing horizon attributes Open Soil Spectral Library. Filled values enter provenance ledger tagged predicted_spectra. first gap-fill downloads OSSL cache, needs network access.

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Queries SoilGrids “MostProbable WRB” raster around profile coordinates reports spatial distribution reference soil groups – prior sanity-check deterministic classification . Point Raster path URL field SoilGrids GeoTIFF, set local raster demos:
code: options(soilKey.test_raster = "/path/to/a/wrb_raster.tif")

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Monte-Carlo robustness analysis: run perturbs texture, pH organic carbon within typical analytical error, re-classifies. robustness percentage tells often class holds; bar chart shows alternative classes profile flipped . (v0.9.100 tab upgrades provenance-weighted posterior distribution.)

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Downloads self-contained cross-system report (WRB / SiBCS / USDA plus horizon table provenance log) HTML PDF. PDF needs working LaTeX install; missing, app falls back HTML tells .

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: Sets diagnostic engine (soilKey strict thresholds vs. aqp regional tolerance), WRB Tier-3 strict-mode toggle, missing-data policy. choices propagate every classification run app.

title: The soilKey Pro Shiny app
text: app fully local deterministic. taxonomic key canonical YAML + R; optional Photo tab can call external model, explicitly configure live provider. classic app unchanged remains available simplest CSV-, results-workflow.

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: Three things never happen : taxonomic key never delegated model; quantitative non-colour attributes never read photograph (hard prompt-level rule); SoilGrids prior never silently displaces measured value – PedonRecord authority order forbids .
code: profile photo  --VLM-->  Munsell colour per horizon field sheet    --VLM-->  site metadata (optional) GPS fix        --SoilGrids-->  depth-resolved clay / sand / silt / pH / OC / CEC                          |                          v               assembled PedonRecord                          |                          v         WRB 2022  +  SiBCS 5  +  USDA ST 13

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: images can also plain character vector profile photos field sheet:
code: library(soilKey)  res <- classify_from_photos(   images   = list(profile = "perfil.jpg", fieldsheet = "ficha.jpg"),   lat      = -22.74,   lon      = -43.68,   country  = "BR",   provider = ellmer::chat_anthropic()   # any ellmer chat object )  res$wrb$name            # e.g. "Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, ...)" res$wrb$evidence_grade  # "D" -- VLM-extracted; or "C" with a prior res$summary             # one row per system res <- classify_from_photos("perfil.jpg", lat = -22.74, lon = -43.68,                             provider = ellmer::chat_anthropic())

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: deliberately default provider. Passing real classification back canned data accident worse error, must supply either live ellmer chat object MockVLMProvider testing:
code: # Testing / offline: a mock provider returning a canned, schema-valid response mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(responses = list(my_canned_munsell_json)) classify_from_photos("perfil.jpg", lat = -22.7, lon = -43.6, provider = mock)

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: Munsell colour alone rarely keys past data-poor catch-(Regosols / Neossolos / Entisols). classify_from_photos() therefore back-fills missing horizon attributes SoilGrids depth prior, default. apply_soilgrids_depth_prior() work can called : horizon value interpolated mid-depth 0-5 / 5-15 / 15-30 / 30-60 / 60-100 / 100-200 cm slices recorded source = "inferred_prior". Skip soilgrids = FALSE want Munsell-driven result.
code: p <- make_cambisol_canonical() p$horizons$clay_pct <- NA_real_  # Live: fetch the six SoilGrids 2.0 depth slices via the ISRIC REST API. apply_soilgrids_depth_prior(p)  # Offline / reproducible: pass the six-slice profiles directly. apply_soilgrids_depth_prior(   p,   depth_profiles = list(clay_pct = c(18, 20, 24, 28, 30, 30)))

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: every value came photograph prior, classification’s evidence grade low construction – D VLM-extracted attribute used, C SoilGrids prior contributed. cell--cell view use compute_per_attribute_evidence_grade(): five-grade scale : measured, B spectra-predicted, C prior-inferred, D VLM-extracted, E user-assumed. photo-classification never report grade B – point: grade tells user exactly much trust result earned.
code: grades <- compute_per_attribute_evidence_grade(res$pedon) grades            # data.table(horizon_idx, attribute, grade)

title: Field-photo-only classification
text: classify_from_photos() built reconnaissance: quick, georeferenced, multi-system first guess field visit, confirmed later description sampling. handful horizons can see, colour can estimate, regional prior can fetch genuinely informative – evidence grade honest headline, never .

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: classification_robustness() (since v0.9.42) perturbs every horizon cell fixed fraction reports one number: share Monte-Carlo runs kept baseline class. treats lab-measured clay value photo-guessed one equally trustworthy. classify_with_uncertainty() two things differently: returns whole posterior – P(class = x) every class profile reached – just single robustness percentage. weights perturbation provenance ledger. (horizon, attribute) cell perturbed amount set evidence grade: (pH Munsell columns get additive half-widths instead; see get_perturbation_scale().) classification rests assumptions reported genuinely uncertain, one resting measurements reported firm – even sit distance key boundary.

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: fields returned soilkey_uncertainty object:
code: library(soilKey)  p <- make_ferralsol_canonical() u <- classify_with_uncertainty(p, n = 200, system = "wrb2022") u #> <soilkey_uncertainty>  system=wrb2022  level=rsg #>   baseline    : Ferralsols #>   MC runs     : 200 (200 successful) #>   entropy     : 0.000 #>   posterior   : #>     Ferralsols                       100.0% #>   most decisive attribute: ... u$posterior     # named numeric vector, P(class), sums to 1 u$top1          # the modal class u$entropy       # Shannon entropy -- 0 for a certain result u$sensitivity   # data.table(attribute, importance)

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: profile, classified twice – clay values measured, merely assumed: Nothing soil changed; know . exactly pedometric uncertainty estimate capture.
code: # Mark every clay value as a bare assumption (grade E). p_assumed <- make_ferralsol_canonical() for (i in seq_len(nrow(p_assumed$horizons))) {   p_assumed$add_measurement(i, "clay_pct",                             p_assumed$horizons$clay_pct[i],                             source = "user_assumed", confidence = 0.2,                             overwrite = TRUE) }  classify_with_uncertainty(p,         n = 200)$entropy   # low  -- measured classify_with_uncertainty(p_assumed, n = 200)$entropy   # high -- assumed

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: classify_with_uncertainty() also runs leave-one-attribute-pass: attribute held fixed turn, drop flip rate measures much attribute drives instability. top row measurement sharpen classification – direct, defensible answer “measure next?”.
code: u$sensitivity #>      attribute importance #> 1     clay_pct      0.18 #> 2     cec_cmol      0.06 #> ...

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: classification_robustness() unchanged default. gains one new argument, provenance_aware, default:
code: # Identical to every previous release: classification_robustness(p, system = "wrb2022", n = 100)  # Opt in to grade-scaled perturbation: classification_robustness(p, system = "wrb2022", n = 100,                           provenance_aware = TRUE)

title: Provenance-weighted classification uncertainty
text: Uncertainty tab Pro Shiny app (run_classify_app()) wraps : pick system, set number Monte-Carlo runs, read posterior bar chart, entropy, attribute-sensitivity table.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Automated soil profile classification WRB 2022 (4th ed.), USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th ed.), Brazilian SiBCS (5th ed.). three systems wired end--end, deepest categorical level, pure R driven versioned YAML rules. Multimodal extraction, spatial priors, OSSL spectroscopy, explicit per-attribute provenance — without ever delegating taxonomic key language model.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Legend: ✅ shipped · 🟡 queue · 🔵 idea / roadmap

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: canonical Brazilian Latossolo Vermelho tropical gneiss, classified end--end across three canonical systems deepest level: WRB delivers complete Chapter 6 name — four principal qualifiers + five supplementary qualifiers canonical order, optional depth specifiers (Epi-/Endo-/Bathy-/…, via classify_wrb2022(specifiers = TRUE)). SiBCS descends four hierarchical levels (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) plus 5th-level Family 15 orthogonal adjectival dimensions. USDA Soil Taxonomy walks complete Path C (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) per Keys Soil Taxonomy 13th ed., plus 5th-level family modifiers (include_family = TRUE). three keys deterministic R code driven versioned YAML rules.
code: library(soilKey)  pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical()  # WRB 2022 — full Chapter 6 name (RSG + qualifiers + specifiers) classify_wrb2022(pedon)$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # SiBCS 5 — 4th level (Subgroup) + Family (5th level) classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE)$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos, argilosa, moderado"  # USDA Soil Taxonomy 13 — Order -> Suborder -> Great Group -> Subgroup -> Family classify_usda(pedon, include_family = TRUE)$name #> [1] "fine, kaolinitic, isohyperthermic Rhodic Hapludox"

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.109 — CRAN release hardening. readiness audit found full R CMD check clean CI didn’t pass ---cran; ---cran, 545 exported topics lacked \value section (likely CRAN rejection). ~600 atomic taxonomic-engine predicates (qual_*, *_usda gates, carater_* / horizonte_*) now @keywords internal — still exported callable, public reference index, trimming documented API ~910 ~195 topics; remaining ~85 public topics gained \value. package now passes R CMD check ---cran 0 errors / 0 warnings. Runnable \examples added entry points, CI now runs ---cran + pkgdown::check_pkgdown(), release metadata (cran-comments, CITATION.cff, lifecycle → maturing) refreshed. user-visible behaviour changed.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.108 — Pro app polish. professional Shiny app (run_classify_app(ui = "pro")) gets thorough UX pass — last three follow-fronts (benchmarks → accuracy → app). soil-science theme (topsoil-brown / terracotta / moss palette flatly, plus slim www/soilkey.css) navbar wordmark give identity; global pedon ribbon keeps active profile (id, horizons, coordinates, build status) visible every tab; “Getting started” Help modal offers one-click Load example & classify builds canonical Ferralsol real Pedon flow jumps straight results. Spectra tab plots attached Vis-NIR spectrum (one trace per horizon) Photo tab previews uploaded image VLM confidence evidence badge. Coordinates range-validated pedon built, USDA-family WRB-depth-specifier toggles surfaced Classify sidebar (two-way-synced Settings), Report now honours : report(pedon, include_family = TRUE, specifiers = TRUE) forwards flags keys. two new report() arguments default FALSE, output stays byte-identical unless opted . new dependencies.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.105 — WRB depth specifiers. classify_wrb2022(pedon, specifiers = TRUE) auto-attaches WRB 2022 Chapter 5 depth specifiers (Epi-/Endo-/Bathy-/Amphi-/Panto-/Kato-) depth-anchored qualifiers, computed diagnostic feature’s actual depth — e.g. gleyic feature confined 50–100 cm becomes Endogleyic. engine existed since v0.9.2.B fired already-prefixed names; wires automatic computation. Applied subsurface qualifiers (epipedons like Mollic/Umbric excluded — depth definitional). Default specifiers = FALSE keeps canonical names byte-identical (verified across every canonical fixture). Exposed classify_all() via Settings toggle Pro app.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.104 — USDA family (5th level). USDA Soil Taxonomy now reaches deepest formal category: classify_usda(pedon, include_family = TRUE) prepends family modifiers subgroup, e.g. “fine, kaolinitic, isohyperthermic Rhodic Hapludox”. Like SiBCS familia, family computed, keyed — six orthogonal dimensions (particle-size, mineralogy via compute_ki/compute_kr, CEC-activity, reaction, temperature regime, depth), FamilyAttribute carrying evidence missing fields. soil temperature regime uses site$soil_temperature_regime present, else infers latitude/elevation (flagged inferred). classify_all(include_family = TRUE) Settings toggle Pro app expose . default (include_family = FALSE) byte-identical . three systems now classify deepest formal level.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: mapping roadmap, complete — Pro Shiny app’s three cartographic surfaces. v0.9.101 — Interactive map tab. Pro Shiny app gains ninth tab, Map: interactive leaflet surface click place point query SoilGrids class prior location via soil_classes_at_location(). tab renders queried buffer, ranked class distribution (WRB 2022 / USDA ST 13 / SiBCS 5), canonical typical-attribute table — works without built pedon (map click built pedon’s coordinate stay sync). Phase 1 mapping roadmap. Adds leaflet Suggests. v0.9.102 — Batch soil map. Map tab gains Batch classify sub-tab: classify many profiles map class. Point sources demo fixtures spread across Brazil (zero-data demo) uploaded long-format CSV (one row per horizon, grouped id one PedonRecord ). Every profile runs classify_all(); points drawn leaflet map coloured reference soil group / order legend per-point popups, listed summary table, exportable GeoPackage via sf. Phase 2 — genuine pedon-scale soil map, point backed deterministic classification. v0.9.103 — Gridded prediction (DSM). Map tab gains Grid prediction sub-tab produces raster class map bounding box, via three selectable methods: () SoilGrids covariates + key — sample SoilGrids covariates per cell, build pseudo-pedon run deterministic key (differentiator: SoilGrids MostProbable layer predicts class ML, applies key); (b) interpolate points — nearest-neighbour Phase-2 classified points; (c) SoilGrids overlay — MostProbable WRB raster comparison. result summarised class exportable GeoTIFF (terra). Phase 3 — completing mapping roadmap. Per §14 ARCHITECTURE.md, covariate method framed complementary , replacement , pixel-scale DSM.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Four sequential roadmap releases turn every previously pending README item shipped functionality. v0.9.97 — Professional Shiny app. run_classify_app() launches eight-tab bslib interface: interactive pedon builder (44 canonical fixtures, editable horizon table, live plotly depth profile), tri-system classification full key trace, VLM photo extraction, OSSL spectral gap-fill, SoilGrids spatial prior, Monte-Carlo uncertainty, downloadable HTML / PDF report. interface bilingual (English / Portuguese) report follows chosen language. legacy single-page “classic” uploader retired v0.9.117 (ui = "classic" now warns launches Pro app). v0.9.98 — WRB Tier-3 strict mode. classify_wrb2022(strict = TRUE) strengthens seven Tier-2 RSG gates (Vertisols / Andosols / Gleysols / Planosols / Ferralsols / Chernozems / Kastanozems) toward canonical WRB 2022 Chapter 4 intent — e.g. Vertisol overlying-clay floor rises 30 → 35 %, Chernozem base-saturation floor 50 → 80 %. strict = FALSE (default) byte-identical v0.9.97; every canonical fixture classifies modes. v0.9.99 — Field-photo-classification. classify_from_photos() assembles PedonRecord entirely VLM extraction field photographs (Munsell colour per horizon + optional site metadata), back-fills missing attributes SoilGrids depth prior, runs three keys. Companion exports apply_soilgrids_depth_prior() (depth-resolved companion spatial_prior_soilgrids()) compute_per_attribute_evidence_grade() (per-cell –E breakdown). Evidence grade E (user-assumed) split D wholly assumed value distinguishable VLM-extracted one. v0.9.100 — Provenance-weighted uncertainty. classify_with_uncertainty() returns probabilistic class distribution Monte-Carlo perturbation provenance ledger — (horizon, attribute) cell perturbed amount scaled evidence grade (measured ±3 %, B spectra ±7 %, C prior ±10 %, D VLM ±17 %, E assumed ±30 %). result soilkey_uncertainty object: posterior P(class), Shannon entropy, leave-one-attribute-sensitivity ranking answers “measure next?”. classification_robustness(provenance_aware = TRUE) exposes weighting v0.9.42 API.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: v0.9.81 → v0.9.96 release series ships 17 surgical fixes across WRB 2022, SiBCS 5, USDA Soil Taxonomy 13 keys, plus CRAN-readiness polish pass. Default canonical behaviour bit--bit preserved every release; one option (soilKey.diagnostic_engine = "aqp") auto-bundles data-quality-aware paths. Cumulative empirical lift five external datasets (post-v0.9.95): Plus v0.9.81 honest 4-level Redape benchmark: Suborder 30.9% → 39.4%, Great Group 29.1% → 35.2%, Subgroup 15.1% → 25.0%. Highlights release series (full per-release diff NEWS.md): v0.9.81 — benchmark_redape() now actually computes Suborder / Great Group / Subgroup accuracy. v0.9.82 — LUCAS Stage 3 rerun: 0% → 60% accuracy v0.9.66+72+77+78+79+80 stack SoilGrids subsoil fill. v0.9.84 — spodic() engine-aware OC-translocation path: KSSL+NASIS Spodosols 1/14 → 5/14. v0.9.85 — andosol() buried-exclusion + andic OC+BD proxy thickness extension. AfSP Andosols 0/5 → 2/5. v0.9.86 / v0.9.89 / v0.9.90 — engine="aqp" auto-bundles v0.9.69 ECEC fallback, v0.9.70 texture-morphological fallback, v0.9.90 argic designation-inference fallback. BDsolos RJ Latossolos 14.9% → 28.1%, Order 40.3% → 46.6%. v0.9.91 — Strict [[reference_wrb]] access bundled WoSIS / KSSL / KSSL+NASIS caches (sidesteps R’s $-partial-matching footgun). v0.9.92 → v0.9.95 — CRAN-readiness: clean R CMD check ---cran, lazy-fetch architecture brings source tarball 10 MB 6 MB. v0.9.96 — README overhaul (release): full English rewrite, expanded implementation-status table, refreshed citations.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: public, maintained, end--end implementation three major soil classification systems. WRB acknowledges (4th-edition preface) internal classification algorithms exist within IUSS Working Group released. U.S. SoilTaxonomy package CRAN provides lookup tables key. zero public software SiBCS language — soilKey. soilKey closes gap three principles: taxonomic key never delegated language model. LLMs restricted schema-validated extraction. Every classification deterministic walk versioned YAML rules full decision trace. Every value carries provenance tag. measured · predicted_spectra · extracted_vlm · inferred_prior · user_assumed. result’s evidence grade (–D) summarises log callers always know robust classification . Side modules never overrule key. Spatial priors flag inconsistencies silently change assigned RSG; spectral predictions fill missing attributes explicit confidence; multimodal extraction pulls structured data without writing class names.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Module 1 (key) side modules (extraction / spectra / spatial) independent. profile spectra still classifies; profile full lab data still benefits spatial-prior consistency check.
code:
flowchart TB subgraph M2["Module 2 — Multimodal extraction"] A[PDF · Field report] --> V(VLM via ellmer) B[Profile photo] --> V C[Field sheet] --> V V --> J["JSON-Schema
validation + retry"] end subgraph M4["Module 4 — Spectra"] K[Vis-NIR / SWIR / MIR] --> O("OSSL prediction
MBL · PLSR-local · pretrained") O --> P["PI95 → confidence"] end subgraph M3["Module 3 — Spatial prior"] S[SoilGrids WCS] --> R(("P(RSG)")) EM[National soil map] --> R end J --> PR["PedonRecord
(provenance log)"] P --> PR PR --> M1["Module 1 — Taxonomic keys"] M1 --> W["WRB 2022 key
32 RSGs · Ch 4–6 (qualifiers + specifiers)"] M1 --> SC["SiBCS 5 key
13 Orders · 44 Suborders · 192 GG · 938 SG · Family"] M1 --> U["USDA ST 13
12 Orders · 68 Suborders · 339 GG · 1288 SG"] W --> CR["ClassificationResult
name · trace · evidence grade"] SC --> CR U --> CR R -.consistency check.-> CR

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey faithfully reproduces three canonical books, versioned YAML rules cross-referencing page numbers diagnostic qualifier definition.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Optional benchmark caches (4 datasets × ~1 MB ) downloaded demand first call load_*_sample() function. prefetch user cache:
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("HugoMachadoRodrigues/soilKey")  # Or via devtools # install.packages("devtools") devtools::install_github("HugoMachadoRodrigues/soilKey") soilKey::download_extdata_cache("all")

what: 1. Build a PedonRecord from horizon data
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: library(soilKey)  hz <- data.table::data.table(   top_cm    = c(0,    20,   55,   115),   bottom_cm = c(20,   55,   115,  200),   designation = c("Ap", "AB", "Bw1", "Bw2"),   munsell_hue_moist    = c("10YR","7.5YR","2.5YR","2.5YR"),   munsell_value_moist  = c(4, 4, 3, 3),   munsell_chroma_moist = c(3, 5, 6, 6),   clay_pct = c(35, 45, 65, 65),   sand_pct = c(25, 20, 15, 15),   silt_pct = c(40, 35, 20, 20),   cec_cmolc_kg = c(8, 6, 5, 4),   bs_pct  = c(35, 30, 25, 20),   oc_pct  = c(2.0, 1.0, 0.5, 0.3),   ph_h2o  = c(5.0, 5.2, 5.3, 5.4),   bulk_density_g_cm3 = c(1.0, 1.1, 1.2, 1.2) ) hz <- ensure_horizon_schema(hz)  pedon <- PedonRecord$new(   site = list(id = "demo-001", lat = -22.4, lon = -43.7, country = "BR"),   horizons = hz )

what: 2. Classify across three systems in one pass
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # WRB 2022 — full Chapter 6 name classify_wrb2022(pedon)$name  # SiBCS 5 — 4th level (Subgroup) + 5th level (Family) classify_sibcs(pedon, include_familia = TRUE)$name  # USDA Soil Taxonomy 13 — Subgroup classify_usda(pedon)$name

what: 3. Inspect the trace and evidence grade
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: res <- classify_wrb2022(pedon) res$evidence_grade   # one of "A", "B", "C", "D" res$trace            # full decision walk: which RSGs were tested, why each failed/passed res$missing_data     # attributes the key wanted but couldn't find res$ambiguities      # alternative classifications still viable on the data

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # Vis-NIR spectrum per horizon, OSSL backbone: pr <- predict_horizon_attributes(   pedon,   spectra      = list(Ap = vnir_ap, Bw1 = vnir_bw1, Bw2 = vnir_bw2),   models       = c("clay_pct", "oc_pct", "cec_cmolc_kg"),   ossl_engine  = "PLSR-local" ) # Each filled attribute carries provenance = "predicted_spectra" + PI95 confidence. # Now classify_wrb2022(pr)$evidence_grade may be "B" (predicted_spectra) # instead of "A" (measured) — provenance survives.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # SoilGrids 250 m WCS at the site coordinates: prior <- spatial_prior(pedon, source = "soilgrids") res   <- classify_wrb2022(pedon, prior = prior) res$prior_check # If the assigned RSG is inconsistent with the SoilGrids posterior, # `res$warnings` flags it. The prior never overrides the key.

what: 6. Render a self-contained report (HTML or PDF)
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
code: # All three results in a single one-pager (HTML, no external deps): classify_all_to_html(pedon, output_file = "demo-001.html")  # Or pass an explicit list of results: classify_all_to_html(   list(     wrb   = classify_wrb2022(pedon),     sibcs = classify_sibcs(pedon),     usda  = classify_usda(pedon)   ),   output_file = "demo-001.html" )  # PDF (requires rmarkdown + LaTeX): classify_all_to_pdf(pedon, output_file = "demo-001.pdf")

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey ships eleven benchmark drivers inst/benchmarks/. post-v0.9.95 cumulative sweep five external datasets (reproduced clean session inst/benchmarks/run_v0987_post_086_sweep.R ~30 seconds, plus LUCAS Stage 3 SoilGrids fill ~60 minutes v0.9.82 RDS):

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: 26 hand-built canonical fixtures (one per WRB Reference Soil Group, sourced WRB 2022 didactic exemplars + ISRIC ISMC monoliths + Soil Atlas Europe) achieve WRB 26 / 26, SiBCS 20 / 20, USDA 26 / 26 every release. Runs offline <2 s; gated every PR.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: NCSS Lab Data Mart joined companion NASIS Morphological sqlite. n = 99 profiles; full four-level USDA hierarchy (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup) measured. WRB 2022 cross-walk via IUSS WRB 2022 Annex 6 yields 24.2% Order accuracy engine = "aqp" (vs 21.2% canonical). v0.9.84 spodic OC-translocation lifts spodic-test recall KSSL+NASIS Podzols 1/14 5/14.

what: 3. Embrapa Redape (curated SiBCS gold standard, Vaz et al. 2023)
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: 96-profile curated GeoTab dataset published Vaz, Silva Jr & Silva Neto (2023) Embrapa Redape repository (DOI 10.48432/PYKKA7). Pedologists hand-reviewed every profile, making gold-standard benchmark SiBCS classification. v0.9.81 wires honest 4-level accuracy:

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: ISRIC WoSIS bundled cache; n = 130 profiles balanced across 26 WRB Reference Soil Groups (5 per RSG). v0.9.88 fixed loader’s reference-field aliasing; v0.9.91 hardened R’s $-partial-matching footgun. Default canonical 17.7%, engine = "aqp" 18.5%.

what: 5. AfSP — ISRIC Africa Soil Profiles Database v1.2
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: n = 120 African profiles. Default 21.7% Order accuracy; engine = "aqp" + andic_oc_bd_proxy + extension: 30.8% (+9.1pp). v0.9.85 lifts AfSP Andosols 0/5 → 2/5 relaxing buried-diagnostic exclusion (per WRB 2022 Ch 4 p 104).

what: 6. LUCAS 2018 — EU topsoil + SoilGrids subsoil fill
title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: n = 30 (FR / PL / , seed 20260508). Stage 3 (engine = "aqp" + full opt-stack + SoilGrids 30–60 cm subsoil fill) reaches 60.0% accuracy, 100% recall Cambisols (18 / 18). Stage 1 / 2 (fill) sit 0% — LUCAS topsoil-horizons satisfy cambic / argic / spodic depth requirements without synthesised subsoil.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: VLM extracts JSON-Schema-validated PedonRecord field-report PDF (photo); deterministic key takes . schema rejects LLM hallucination class names — extraction restricted per-attribute observations.
code: # One-liner. Local-first; no API key needed; data never leaves your machine. pedon <- extract_pedon_from_pdf(   "field_survey_2024.pdf",   vlm_engine = ellmer::chat_ollama("gemma3:4b") )  classify_wrb2022(pedon)$name #> [1] "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Vignettes: 10+ vignettes vignettes/ covering getting-started, end--end classification, cross-system correlation, VLM extraction, spatial + spectra pipeline, WoSIS benchmark, KSSL+NASIS multi-level, fully-worked Embrapa profile. pkgdown reference site: hugomachadorodrigues.github.io/soilKey — every exported function full API docs runnable examples. Architecture document: ARCHITECTURE.md — full design rationale, module separation, v1.0 roadmap. Per-release diff: NEWS.md — every fix, every benchmark uplift, every test added.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: Every attribute PedonRecord carries provenance tag: ClassificationResult$evidence_grade (–D) summarises trace: — every attribute key consulted measured. B — every attribute measured predicted_spectra PI95 ≤ threshold. C — least one attribute extracted_vlm VLM-confidence ≤ 0.85. D — least one attribute inferred_prior user_assumed.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: soilKey developed Universidade Federal Rural Rio de Janeiro (UFRRJ), Departamento de Solos. benchmark datasets generously made public ISRIC (AfSP, WoSIS), USDA-NRCS (KSSL Lab Data Mart, NASIS Morphological), European Soil Data Centre (LUCAS), Embrapa (BDsolos, Redape, SmartSolos Expert API), FEBR consortium (UFSM). deterministic-key separation inspired IUSS Working Group WRB’s stated commitment open taxonomic logic. Special thanks Glauber José Vaz colleagues Embrapa opening SmartSolos Expert REST API curating Redape gold-standard SiBCS dataset — directly enable soilKey cross-validation benchmark axes Brazilian system.

title: soilKey -- Automated soil profile classification
text: MIT © 2026 Hugo Rodrigues. CRAN-style template LICENSE; full text LICENSE.md. package source MIT. bundled benchmark caches retain respective upstream licenses (ISRIC AfSP / WoSIS public-domain; NCSS Lab Data Mart public-domain US Federal data). Redape SiBCS dataset published Vaz et al. (2023) original repository terms — see DOI details. Status (v0.9.96, 2026-05-09): CRAN-submit-ready. R CMD check ---cran returns 0 errors / 0 warnings / 2 trivial NOTEs. seven CI matrix runs (macOS, Ubuntu × 3 R versions, Windows, pkgdown, test-coverage) green every PR merged main since v0.9.65. three classification systems wired end--end deepest categorical level. WRB 2022 (32 RSGs + 229 234 canonical qualifiers deliverable, 5 honest gaps), SiBCS 5 (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup → Family; 13 / 44 / 192 / 938 registered classes), USDA Soil Taxonomy 13 (Order → Suborder → Great Group → Subgroup, 339/339 great groups + 2 003 2 715 subgroups = 73.8%; run coverage_report() live -name diff every level). DOI: https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19930112 (resolves latest version Zenodo). Per-release changes NEWS.md; roadmap ARCHITECTURE.md; CRAN submission instructions inst/cran-submission/HOW_TO_SUBMIT.md.

what: Horizonte B espodico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 62-65; v0.7) — B_espodico
title: Horizonte B espodico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 62-65; v0.7) — B_espodico
text: Subsuperficial com acumulo iluvial de Al + Fe + materia organica; espessura \>= 2.5 cm. Tipos: Bs, Bhs, Bh, ortstein. Reuso de spodic (WRB) que ja codifica criterios essencialmente identicos.

title: Horizonte B incipiente (SiBCS Cap 2, p 59-61; v0.7) — B_incipiente
text: Subsuperficial sob /Ap/AB com alteracao fisica e quimica incipiente, NAO satisfazendo B textural / latossolico / nitico / espodico / planico, com: espessura \>= 10 cm; textura francoarenosa ou mais fina; < 50% estrutura da rocha original; evidencias de pedogenese (cor mais viva remocao de         carbonatos designation Bw/Bi); NAO satisfaz: argic, ferralic, espodic, planic, e nao tem         duripa/petrocalcico/fragipa.

what: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
title: Horizonte B latossolico (SiBCS Cap 2, p 57-59; v0.7 strict) — B_latossolico
text: Adicionalmente ferralic (WRB), o B latossolico SiBCS exige: Espessura minima de 50 cm; Textura francoarenosa ou mais fina; Estrutura granular muito pequena/pequena ou em blocos         subangulares fraco/moderado; < 5% volume mostrando estrutura da rocha original; Ki \<= 2.2 (geralmente \<= 2.0); Cerosidade maximo pouca e fraca. v0.7 enforce thickness, texture, e ausencia de estrutura primaria herdada via designation e clay; Ki/Kr quantitativos sao v0.8 (precisa de SiO2/Al2O3 lab-data nao schema).

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: ClassificationResult: structured outcome running key ClassificationResult: structured outcome running key

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: Returned classify_wrb2022 (future classify_sibcs). Carries full decision trace — RSGs tested, passed, failed, indeterminate missing data — plus assigned class, qualifiers, ambiguities (RSGs nearly satisfied), missing data refine result, provenance-aware evidence grade, biogeographical prior-based warnings.

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: system Character. "WRB 2022" "SiBCS 5". name Character. Full classification name qualifiers (e.g. "Rhodic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric)"). rsg_or_order Character. Bare RSG (WRB) order (SiBCS), e.g. "Ferralsols". qualifiers List. Principal supplementary qualifiers canonical order. trace List. One element per RSG tested (key order), code, name, passed, evidence, missing. ambiguities List. RSGs came close passing — useful hints follow-measurements. missing_data Character vector. Attributes whose measurement refine resolve result. evidence_grade Character. "" (measured), "B" (spectra-predicted), "C" (prior-inferred), "D" (VLM-extracted), "E" (user-assumed), NA_character_. prior_check List NULL. Result spatial-prior sanity check (consistent / inconsistent / run). warnings Character vector. Free-form warnings.

title: ClassificationResult: structured outcome of running a key — ClassificationResult
text: ClassificationResult$new() ClassificationResult$print() ClassificationResult$summary() ClassificationResult$report() ClassificationResult$clone()

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome diagnostic test DiagnosticResult: structured outcome diagnostic test

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult
text: Returned every WRB SiBCS diagnostic function (e.g. argic, ferralic, mollic). DiagnosticResult never reduces bare TRUE/FALSE — always carries () layers satisfied criteria, (b) per-sub-test evidence, (c) attributes required missing, (d) literature reference diagnostic definition. passed TRUE/FALSE/NA; NA means test evaluated critical attributes missing. three-valued semantics propagates rule engine — indeterminate test silently fail.

title: DiagnosticResult: structured outcome of a diagnostic test — DiagnosticResult

Sub-test results, list least passed, layers, missing. missing Character vector. Attribute names needed NA. reference Character. Literature citation diagnostic. notes Character. Free-form notes (interpretation choices, edge cases hit).

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: stand-ellmer chat object. Exposes $chat(prompt, ...) method, instead calling model pops next response pre-loaded queue. Designed testthat unit tests exercise extraction logic without API keys network access. call $chat() returns next element responses list. call number matches validation_error_at, response replaced deliberately malformed JSON string, allowing tests exercise retry--validation-failure path implemented validate_or_retry.

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
code: good_json <- '{"horizons": [...]}' mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(responses = list(good_json)) result <- mock$chat("any prompt")  # returns good_json  # Simulate one validation error before success. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list("not really json", good_json),   validation_error_at = NULL  # already invalid as-is )  # Or force an attempt to be invalid via the helper. mock <- MockVLMProvider$new(   responses = list(good_json, good_json),   validation_error_at = 1L )

title: Mock VLM provider for testing — MockVLMProvider
text: use, mock exposes $call_count (integer) $prompts_received (list every prompt passed $chat()), lets tests assert retry prompts include previous validation error.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: R6 object class PedonRecord.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: central data carrier soilKey. PedonRecord bundles everything know one soil profile: site metadata, horizons table (fixed canonical schema — see horizon_column_spec), optional Vis-NIR/MIR spectra, profile photographs, source documents, provenance log records, per (horizon, attribute) pair, value came (measured, extracted_vlm, predicted_spectra, inferred_prior, user_assumed). diagnostic functions (argic, ferralic, mollic, ...) consume PedonRecord directly. provenance log allows final ClassificationResult assign meaningful evidence grade.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: site List. Site-level metadata: lat, lon, crs (default 4326), date, country, elevation_m, slope_pct, aspect_deg, landform, parent_material, land_use, vegetation, drainage_class, plus arbitrary id. horizons data.table canonical horizon schema. spectra List optional vnir matrix (rows = horizons, cols = wavelengths nm), mir matrix, metadata list. images List named lists describing profile photographs. documents List named lists describing source documents. provenance data.table columns horizon_idx, attribute, source, confidence, notes.

title: PedonRecord: structured representation of a single pedon — PedonRecord
text: Validate record soil-physical sanity rules. Checks: top < bottom every horizon; overlapping depths; clay+silt+sand sum 100 ± 2 three reported; pH values plausible (1..12); CEC >= sum exchangeable bases (Ca, Mg, K, Na); Munsell value/chroma plausible ranges; coarse fragments percent [0, 100]; OC geographic ranges. Returns list valid, errors, warnings, n_horizons.
