title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: Tests andic property complex – volcanic-ash-derived allophanic / imogolitic / Al-humus material. Diagnostic Andosols. Two alternative qualifying paths per WRB 2022 Ch 3.2: Al-Fe oxalate + low BD:         (Al_ox + 0.5*Fe_ox) >= min_alfe (default 2.0%)         bulk_density <= max_bd (default 0.9 g/cm^3)         layer. Phosphate retention: phosphate_retention_pct         >= min_p_retention (default 70%). Either path qualifies. volcanic-glass criterion separate vitric_properties diagnostic; Andosols key (andic vitric) RSG-gate level (andosol).

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
code: andic_properties(   pedon,   min_alfe = 2,   max_bd = 0.9,   min_p_retention = 70,   min_oc_proxy = 4,   max_bd_proxy = 0.9 )

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_alfe Minimum (Al_ox + 0.5*Fe_ox) percent Al-Fe path (default 2.0). max_bd Maximum bulk density g/cm^3 Al-Fe path (default 0.9). min_p_retention Minimum phosphate retention % P path (default 70). min_oc_proxy Minimum SOC % v0.9.80 OC+BD proxy path (default 4.0). consulted proxy enabled via options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE). max_bd_proxy Maximum bulk density g/cm^3 v0.9.80 OC+BD proxy path (default 0.9). consulted proxy enabled.

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: Field-described volcanic-ash soils (e.g.\ AfSP, KSSL/NASIS, SOTER) routinely lack oxalate Al/Fe phosphate retention measurements, canonical paths return NA Andosols cascade RSGs. genetic signature still detectable coarser data: high SOC (>= 4-5%) plus low bulk density (<= 0.9 g/cm^3) typical allophanic / Al-humus complexation. options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE) function adds third path fires canonical paths fail surface horizon shows oc_pct >= min_oc_proxy bulk_density_g_cm3 <= max_bd_proxy (OC alone >= 5% BD missing). Default FALSE (canonical behaviour preserved).

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: options(soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy_extend = TRUE) (meaningful soilKey.andic_oc_bd_proxy = TRUE), iteratively extend proxy layers include contiguous deeper layers whose oc_pct >= min_oc_proxy / 2 whose bulk_density_g_cm3 missing <= max_bd_proxy + 0.15. extension stops first horizon failing either constraint, ferralic / argic subsoil accidentally inflate andic thickness. Default FALSE – canonical proxy behaviour preserved.

title: Andic properties (WRB 2022) — andic_properties
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Andic   properties.

text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Numeric threshold option (see Details). max_top_cm Numeric threshold option (see Details). buried_below_cm Numeric: layers exclusion diagnostics whose top_cm \>= depth treated buried exclude Andosol (default 50, per WRB 2022 Ch 4 p 104). strict Logical NULL. NULL (default) resolves via getOption("soilKey.rsg_strict", FALSE). TRUE disables buried-exclusion tolerance.

title: Andosol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 104) — andosol
text: v0.3.4 enforces (1) andic vitric (2) combined thickness \>= 30 cm starting upper 25 cm (3) negative-list exclusions argic / ferralic / plinthic / spodic.

title: Andosol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 104) — andosol
text: WRB 2022 Ch 4 p 104 specifies Andosol exclusion list (argic / ferralic / petroplinthic / pisoplinthic / plinthic / spodic) "<= 100 cm unless buried 50 cm". earlier implementation excluded Andosol whenever diagnostics passed anywhere profile, including layers starting deeper 50 cm – mis-fires AfSP Andosol references like CM W3_0047, argic layer 56-72 cm wrongly excluded andic surface stack. v0.9.85 restricts exclusion check layers starting <= 50 cm: buried argic / ferralic / plinthic / spodic deeper levels longer disqualifies surface andic stack Andosol.

title: Andosol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 104) — andosol
text: strict = TRUE v0.9.85 buried-exclusion tolerance switched : argic / ferralic / plinthic / spodic horizon anywhere profile excludes Andosol, regardless depth.

title: Anthric horizons (WRB 2022) — anthric_horizons
text: Tests five anthropogenic surface horizons recognised WRB 2022 (hortic, irragric, plaggic, pretic, terric). Diagnostic Anthrosols. Two alternative paths qualify: Designation: layer's designation contains one         hortic|irragric|plaggic|pretic|terric. Property-based: surface layer (top_cm <= 5)         least min_thickness_cm cm thick (default 20)         elevated dark colour (Munsell value moist <=         max_munsell_value, default 4) elevated         plant-available P (p_mehlich3_mg_kg >=         min_p_mg_kg, default 50). Either path qualifies.

what: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
text: Composes canonical soil-spectroscopy sequence, step optional applied fixed scientific order: reflectance \(\\) (absorbance) \(\\) (Savitzky-Golay smoothing) \(\\) (Savitzky-Golay 1st 2nd derivative). Savitzky-Golay pass trims (window - 1) / 2 columns edge; wavelength axis trimmed match returned callers can plot treated spectrum correct axis.

title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
code: apply_spectral_preprocessing(   X,   wavelengths = NULL,   absorbance = FALSE,   sg_smooth = FALSE,   sg_derivative = 0L,   window = 11L,   poly = 2L )

title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
text: X Numeric matrix (rows = samples/horizons, columns = wavelengths) numeric vector (treated one sample). wavelengths Optional numeric wavelength axis. Defaults numeric part colnames(X), else 1:ncol(X). absorbance Logical; apply \(= \log_{10}(1 / R)\). sg_smooth Logical; apply Savitzky-Golay smoothing (m = 0). sg_derivative Integer 0, 1 2; Savitzky-Golay derivative order (0 = none). window Odd Savitzky-Golay window (default 11); coerced valid odd value [3, ncol). poly Savitzky-Golay polynomial order (default 2); clamped [1, window - 1].

title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
text: list X (treated numeric matrix, wavelength         column names trimmed match), wavelengths (numeric)         steps (ordered character vector describing         transforms actually applied, starting         "Reflectance").

title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
text: transform robust: reflectance looks like percentage (maximum > 1.5) rescaled 0–1 fraction absorbance log, values clamped away zero avoid log(0), Savitzky-Golay step fit requested window available wavelengths skipped (recorded steps) rather erroring.

title: Apply a step-by-step Vis-NIR / MIR preprocessing pipeline — apply_spectral_preprocessing
code: X <- matrix(seq(0.1, 0.5, length.out = 3 * 60), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) colnames(X) <- seq(400, 2400, length.out = 60) res <- apply_spectral_preprocessing(X, absorbance = TRUE,                                     sg_smooth = TRUE, sg_derivative = 1L) res$steps          # ordered treatment labels #> [1] "Reflectance"                   "Absorbance (log 1/R)"          #> [3] "SG smoothing (w=11, p=2)"      "SG 1st derivative (w=11, p=2)" dim(res$X)         # columns trimmed by the two SG passes #> [1]  3 40

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Tests whether horizon meets argic horizon criteria per Chapter 3 WRB 2022 (4th edition). Argic subsurface horizon distinctly higher clay content overlying horizon, qualified three depth-conditional clay-increase rules; must also texture sandy loam finer, satisfy minimum thickness, exhibit albeluvic glossic features (direct profile Retisol path).

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
code: argic(   pedon,   min_thickness = 7.5,   system = c("wrb2022", "usda"),   engine = NULL,   require_t = NULL )

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 7.5). system One "wrb2022" (default) "usda". Selects clay-increase threshold set: WRB uses 6/1.4/20 pp/ratio/pp; KST 13ed uses 3/1.2/8 (looser). See test_clay_increase_argic table. engine v0.9.63+. One "soilkey" (hand-coded path, default back-compat) "aqp" (canonical NRCS dispatch via aqp::getArgillicBounds). NULL (new default) function reads getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine", "soilkey") global options(soilKey.diagnostic_engine = "aqp") flips every argic() call without modifying call sites. See argic_aqp. require_t v0.9.63+. Forwarded aqp::getArgillicBounds engine = "aqp": TRUE requires "t" suffix horizon designation (strict KST 13ed text); FALSE accepts argic clay-increase alone (permissive data-sparse profiles). NULL (default) auto-picks: TRUE system = "usda", FALSE system = "wrb2022". Ignored engine = "soilkey".

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: Sub-tests called (list passed, layers, missing, details, notes): test_clay_increase_argic – three-pronged         WRB 2022 clay-increase rule. test_minimum_thickness – thickness >= 7.5 cm         (configurable via min_thickness). test_texture_argic – texture sandy loam         finer (silt + 2 * clay >= 30). test_not_albeluvic – excludes profiles glossic         tongues (Retisol path). v0.1 limitations: clay-increase distance (<= 30 cm vertical, <= 15 cm abrupt textural change) yet enforced; scheduled v0.2 depends horizon boundary descriptions.

title: Argic horizon (WRB 2022) — argic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3 – Argic horizon.

what: Argic / argillic horizon via aqp::getArgillicBounds() — argic_aqp
title: Argic / argillic horizon via aqp::getArgillicBounds() — argic_aqp
text: Wraps aqp::getArgillicBounds() (Beaudette et al.) soilKey's DiagnosticResult contract. aqp implementation canonical NRCS R port uses tiered USDA-NRCS clay-increase thresholds: Eluvial clay < 15\ Eluvial clay 15-40\ Eluvial clay \>= 40\ (vs. soilKey's hand-coded argic uses WRB 6/1.4/20 thresholds). BDsolos / FEBR / KSSL profiles aqp rule closer KST 13ed BDsolos field practice.

what: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: KST 13ed argillic horizon requires "evidence illuvial accumulation clay" alongside clay-increase rule. Acceptable evidence: oriented clays bridging sand grains >= 1% horizon; clay films lining pores coating ped faces; lamellae 5 mm thick.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
code: argillic_clay_films_test(pedon)

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: pedon PedonRecord.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: test reads three complementary slots, order evidence strength: pedon$site$nasis_diagnostic_features – NASIS         pediagfeatures.featkind vector. surveyor's         explicit "Argillic horizon" entry directly confirms         clay-illuviation evidence (~13 500 entries 2021         NASIS snapshot). Strongest evidence. pedon$horizons$clay_films_amount – per-horizon         clay-film abundance derived NASIS phpvsf.         Values: "", "common", "many",         "continuous". Direct measurement. pedon$horizons$designation containing 't'         master suffix (e.g. Bt, Btk, Btx,         Bt1, 2Bt). v0.9.28: pedologist         wrote designation explicitly identified         horizon clay-illuvial – per KST 13ed Ch 18, 't'         suffix means "accumulation silicate clay" –         counts positive evidence even NASIS records         absent. unlocks KST 13ed argillic thresholds         ~47          pediagfeatures phpvsf records. three sources counts positive evidence (logical ). passed = NA none populated horizon designation field present (lab-loaders without horizon descriptions). passed = FALSE designations exist none 't' suffix NASIS slots empty.

title: Test for clay-illuviation evidence (KST 13ed Ch 3 p 4) — argillic_clay_films_test
text: Soil Survey Staff (2022), Keys Soil Taxonomy 13th   ed., Ch. 3, argillic horizon (clay-illuviation criteria, p. 4);   Ch. 18, master horizon symbols (t: silicate-clay   accumulation, p. 332).

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: BDsolos densely populates reference_sibcs (~82 v0.9.59 audit) sparsely populates reference_wrb reference_st (UF-dependent; ~5 states). function always reports per-system label coverage ($coverage) caller can judge representative accuracy figure .

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: SiBCS comparison level = "order" default, converts BDsolos -caps Portuguese label (e.g. "ARGISSOLO VERMELHO Tb EUTROFICO ...") soilKey plural Title Case form ("Argissolos") via normalise_febr_sibcs. Set sibcs_level = "subordem" compare first two SiBCS tokens (Ordem + Subordem). WRB USDA comparisons Reference Soil Group / Order level: normalise_febr_wrb() strips qualifier parens pluralises bare RSG ("Xanthic Ferralsol" -> "Ferralsols"); normalise_febr_usda() maps suffix last subgroup token USDA Order ("Typic Haplorthox" -> "Oxisols").

title: Benchmark soilKey classifiers against BDsolos national reference labels — benchmark_bdsolos
text: Pedons without reference label given system silently excluded system's comparison (still classified two systems). system zero pedons reference label, corresponding $per_system entry accuracy = NA_real_ message = "no_reference_labels". Classifier errors caught per-pedon recorded n_errors; abort run.

code: benchmark_lucas_2018(   pedons,   esdb_root,   attribute = "WRBLV1",   fill_texture_from = NULL,   fill_topsoil_from = c("none", "soilgrids", "spectra"),   fill_subsoil_from = c("none", "soilgrids"),   fill_properties = c("clay", "sand", "silt", "phh2o", "soc", "cec", "bdod", "nitrogen",     "cfvo"),   ossl_models = NULL,   classify_with = c("wrb2022", "sibcs"),   max_n = NULL,   soilgrids_lookup_fn = lookup_soilgrids,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Run the LUCAS Soil 2018 / ESDB WRB benchmark — benchmark_lucas_2018
text: pedons List PedonRecord objects, typically load_lucas_soil_2018. esdb_root Path unpacked ESDB raster directory (containing WRBLV1/ sub-folder). attribute ESDB attribute use reference. Default "WRBLV1" (Reference Soil Group, 31 codes). sensible choices: "FAO90LV1" (legacy FAO 1990). fill_texture_from Deprecated alias fill_topsoil_from (v0.9.49 signature). "soilgrids", treated fill_topsoil_from = "soilgrids" fill_properties = c("clay", "sand", "silt") fill_subsoil_from = "none". fill_topsoil_from One "none" (default), "soilgrids" (fills topsoil 0-20 cm SoilGrids 250m 0-5 cm), "spectra" (runs predict_from_spectra supplied ossl_models; pedons must $spectra$vnir attached, e.g. via attach_lucas_spectra). fill_subsoil_from One "none" (default) "soilgrids" (synthesises 30-60 cm B horizon SoilGrids 250m). Unlocks WRB diagnostic horizons depend subsoil features (cambic, argic, mollic). fill_properties Character vector SoilGrids properties fill fill_topsoil_from = "soilgrids" fill_subsoil_from = "soilgrids". Default uses 9 properties: clay, sand, silt, phh2o, soc, cec, bdod, nitrogen, cfvo. Set c("clay", "sand", "silt") recover v0.9.49 behaviour. cfvo mapped coarse_fragments_pct, drives Leptosols diagnostic (>= 90 within 25 cm). ossl_models Required fill_topsoil_from = "spectra". list soilKey_pls_model objects train_pls_from_ossl (v0.9.46). classify_with One "wrb2022" (default) "sibcs". max_n Optional integer cap number pedons benchmarked. Useful quick development runs. soilgrids_lookup_fn Internal: SoilGrids lookup function (defaults lookup_soilgrids). Override unit tests inject deterministic stub. verbose TRUE (default), prints progress.

Comparison via first two normalised tokens         predicted name vs reference; "great_group" (USDA, v0.9.24) – LAST token         subgroup name (e.g. "typic hapludalfs" ->         "hapludalfs"). Isolates whether Great Group         machinery correct independent subgroup modifiers         (Typic / Aquic / Vertic / Cumulic / Pachic / etc.).         Reads site$reference_usda_grtgroup; "suborder" (USDA, v0.9.24) – maps Great         Group prediction canonical Suborder suffix         ("hapludalfs" -> "udalfs") using         KST 13ed Ch 4 ~70-Suborder list. Reads         site$reference_usda_suborder. boot_n Bootstrap replicates CI (default 1000).

Rows aligned reflectance id_col. id_col Sample identifier column shared tables (default "id"). system One "sibcs" (default), "wrb2022", "usda". profile_col Column grouping samples profiles (default id_col). folds Number CV folds (default 5). properties Attributes predict spectra (default fill_from_spectra set). method Spectral model: "mbl", "plsr_local" "pretrained" (passed fill_from_spectra). wavelengths Optional explicit wavelength vector (nm) reflectance columns wavelength-named. resample_to Optional target wavelength grid (nm) linearly resample every spectrum onto (e.g. 350:2500); default keeps native grid. property_map, label_map Optional named lists overriding alias auto-detection, e.g. property_map = list(clay_pct = "ARGILA"). normalize One "auto" (divide 100 values look like percent), "percent", "none". fold_id Optional integer vector (one per profile, sorted-id order) use fixed folds instead deterministic modulo split. verbose Print one-line summary (default TRUE).

title: Unified cross-dataset benchmark across SiBCS / WRB / USDA — benchmark_unified
text: systems Character vector. subset c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"). Default "" runs three. datasets Character vector. subset c("bdsolos", "febr", "kssl", "lucas_esdb"). Default "" pools every dataset reference labels requested systems. Datasets without reference labels system silently excluded system's pooled result. paths Named list dataset paths. Element names match datasets. NULL (default), soilKey looks canonical paths "~/soil_data/". max_n_per_dataset Optional integer cap per-dataset sample size (useful development / debugging). NULL (default) classifies every available pedon. engine Currently forwarded Phase-1 aqp wiring. One "soilkey" (default), "aqp", "". "aqp", sets options(soilKey.diagnostic_engine = "aqp") duration benchmark, routes argic() / cambic() canonical aqp::getArgillicBounds / getCambicBounds. harmonize TRUE (default FALSE), applies harmonize_to_gsm dataset's pedons classification, putting chemistry/texture GSM depth grid (0-5 / 5-15 / 15-30 / 30-60 / 60-100 / 100-200 cm). Required cross-dataset pooling integrity (Phase 2.3) slow (~1-2 min 1k pedons) may degrade per-dataset accuracy slightly splined depths approximations. gapfill FALSE (default), applies gapfill_within_pedon dataset's pedons classification, filling interior NA cells continuous depth-trending attributes within-pedon linear interpolation. Accepts values gapfill argument classify_all (TRUE, character vector attributes, named list). Lets measure /accuracy lift gap-fill reproducibly harness. verbose TRUE (default), emits cli progress.

title: Classify a pedon across all three taxonomic systems — classify_all
text: pedon PedonRecord. systems Character vector. subset c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"), literal "" (default) run every system. on_missing One "warn" (default), "silent", "error". Forwarded verbatim classifier. include_familia Forwarded classify_sibcs (default TRUE). effect systems. include_family Forwarded classify_usda (default FALSE) derive USDA 5th-level family. effect systems. specifiers Forwarded classify_wrb2022 (default FALSE) auto-attach WRB depth specifiers. effect systems. gapfill Forwarded three classifiers (default FALSE => byte-identical). Opt-within-pedon depth gap-fill; see gapfill_within_pedon. Applied independently per system deep copy, caller's pedon never mutated. ... Additional named arguments silently ignored.

title: Classify a pedon across all three taxonomic systems — classify_all

Useful tabulating           many pedons one shot.

what: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: Given Vis-NIR (MIR) spectrum OSSL reference library enriched WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels, returns K spectrally similar profiles plus probabilistic class prediction aggregated labels.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
code: classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum,   ossl_library,   system = c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"),   k = 25L,   preprocess = "snv+sg1",   region = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: spectrum Numeric vector 1-row matrix (query spectrum). Must align (preprocessing) column space ossl_library$Xr. ossl_library list Xr (numeric matrix, rows = OSSL training profiles, cols = wavelengths) Yr (data frame keyed property; must include column named wrb_rsg / sibcs_ordem / usda_order labels aggregate ). ossl_library may also carry lat lon columns Yr regional filter. system One "wrb2022" (default), "sibcs", "usda". Controls label column Yr aggregated. k Number nearest neighbours (default 25). preprocess Pre-processing pipeline; passed preprocess_spectra. Default "snv+sg1". region Optional list(lat, lon, radius_km) regional filter ossl_library$Yr$lat / lon. verbose Emit cli summary.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: list three elements: distribution data.table columns           class, n_neighbours, probability           (= n_neighbours / k), sorted probability. neighbours data.table one row per           neighbour (top K), columns rank, distance,           class, plus columns present           ossl_library$Yr. query query metadata (system, k,           region filter, n_library_rows, n_filtered).

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: **spectral analogy** classifier. replace deterministic key classify_wrb2022 / classify_sibcs / classify_usda; instead provides high-prior "expected class" user lab data, reducing search space collecting confirming attributes.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: default compute distances PLS scores (matching resemble / OSSL recipe), PLS components fit OSSL reference Yr matrix. resemble unavailable, fall back PCA scores stats::prcomp preprocessed Xr – defensible--simpler heuristic.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
text: Optional lat / lon / radius_km arguments filter OSSL library profiles within radius_km (great-circle) query location computing distances. implements "biome-aware" use case architecture document calls : Cerrado profile class inferred spectral neighbours Boreal taiga.

title: Classify a soil by spectral similarity to OSSL reference profiles — classify_by_spectral_neighbours
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Toy run against the bundled demo library (synthetic): data(ossl_demo_sa) # Inject a fake label column for the demo (real OSSL has it): ossl_demo_sa$Yr$wrb_rsg <- sample(c("FR", "AC", "LX", "AL"),                                     nrow(ossl_demo_sa$Yr),                                     replace = TRUE) query <- ossl_demo_sa$Xr[1, ] res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(query, ossl_demo_sa,                                         k = 10) res$distribution    # ranked classes res$neighbours      # the 10 most similar profiles } # }

what: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: Highest-level entry point soilKey VLM pipeline. Given soil-description PDF / profile-wall photograph, function:

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
code: classify_from_documents(   pdf = NULL,   image = NULL,   fieldsheet = NULL,   pedon = NULL,   provider = "auto",   model = NULL,   systems = c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"),   report = NULL,   overwrite = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: pdf Optional path soil-description PDF. image Optional path profile-wall image (JPG / PNG); supplied, Munsell extraction attempted configured provider. fieldsheet Optional path site-metadata field sheet (image PDF). pedon Optional existing PedonRecord; supplied, function fills fields VLM extraction can fill (subject provenance-authority order). provider Either provider name passed vlm_provider (default "ollama") pre-built ellmer chat object (want full control system_prompt, api_key, ...). model Optional model identifier; passed vlm_provider() provider string. Defaults per-provider default default_model. systems Character vector listing classification systems run; subset c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"). Default: three. report Optional output path self-contained report (.html .pdf). supplied, report called classification results + pedon. Default NULL (report file). overwrite merging extracted values existing pedon, allow VLM-extracted attributes clobber already-recorded ones. Default FALSE – provenance authority order (measured > extracted_vlm) enforced PedonRecord$add_measurement(). verbose Emit cli progress messages. Default TRUE.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: list elements: pedon (mutated) PedonRecord. classifications Named list three           ClassificationResult objects keyed           wrb, sibcs, usda. report Path rendered report file (          report = ... supplied), else NULL. provider chat-provider object actually used           (useful downstream debugging cost accounting).

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: Constructs vision-language provider chat object via         vlm_provider (defaults local Ollama         Gemma 4 edge institutional independence data         sovereignty). Extracts horizons pdf via         extract_horizons_from_pdf, Munsell colours         image via         extract_munsell_from_photo, site         metadata fieldsheet via         extract_site_from_fieldsheet. Every         extracted attribute stamped source =         "extracted_vlm" PedonRecord's provenance log. Runs three deterministic keys         (classify_wrb2022,         classify_sibcs,         classify_usda). VLM never classifies –         package's architectural invariant preserved. Optionally renders one-pager HTML / PDF report via         report. least one pdf, image fieldsheet must supplied; can also pass existing partially-filled PedonRecord via pedon let function fill gaps.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: default provider = "ollama" runs entire VLM pipeline user's machine via Gemma 4 (edge variant, ~3 GB, multimodal text+image). part soil description, photograph field sheet ever leaves local network. recommended configuration governmental surveys, indigenous land studies, unpublished research data; also makes pipeline reproducible without internet connection. Cloud providers ("anthropic", "openai", "google") remain one argument away right call.

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
text: VLM never classifies. Every extracted value carries         source = "extracted_vlm"; deterministic keys         consume resulting PedonRecord unaware         value obtained. Provenance preserved end--end.         evidence_grade         ClassificationResult reflects whether decisive         attributes came measured,         predicted_spectra, extracted_vlm,         inferred_prior, user_assumed –         caller always knows robust classification . Authority order enforced. pre-existing         measured value never silently overwritten         later extracted_vlm value (unless         overwrite = TRUE).

title: Build a fully-classified `PedonRecord` from documents in one call — classify_from_documents
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # The simplest possible end-to-end call -- local Gemma 4 edge. res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   image    = "perfil_042_parede.jpg",   report   = "perfil_042.html" ) res$classifications$wrb$name #> "Geric Ferric Rhodic Chromic Ferralsol (Clayic, Humic, Dystric, Ochric, Rubic)"  # Cloud provider for a one-shot, production run res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   provider = "anthropic" )  # Different Gemma 4 size on Ollama res <- classify_from_documents(   pdf      = "perfil_042_descricao.pdf",   provider = "ollama",   model    = "gemma4:31b" ) } # }

what: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
text: -lab-data pipeline: profile photographs sent vision-language model Munsell-colour (optionally) site-metadata extraction; missing horizon attributes back-filled SoilGrids depth prior; WRB 2022, SiBCS 5 USDA Soil Taxonomy keys run assembled PedonRecord.

title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
code: classify_from_photos(   images,   lat = NULL,   lon = NULL,   country = NULL,   provider = NULL,   systems = c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"),   soilgrids = TRUE,   depth_profiles = NULL,   on_missing = "silent" )

title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
text: images Either character vector profile-photo paths, named list elements profile (character vector, required) fieldsheet (character vector, optional). lat, lon Optional decimal-degree coordinates. supplied seed pedon$site used SoilGrids fetch; field sheet can also supply extraction. country Optional ISO-2 country code; passed constructed pedon's site metadata. provider vision-language provider: ellmer chat object live use, MockVLMProvider testing offline demos. Required – default, real classification never produced canned data accident. systems Character vector, subset c("wrb", "sibcs", "usda"). soilgrids TRUE (default) missing horizon attributes back-filled SoilGrids depth prior via apply_soilgrids_depth_prior. depth_profiles Optional named list six-slice SoilGrids depth profiles, forwarded apply_soilgrids_depth_prior. Supplying skips network call. on_missing Forwarded classifiers; default "silent".

title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
text: named list one ClassificationResult per         requested system ($wrb, $sibcs, $usda),         constructed $pedon, $provenance ledger,         one-row $summary data frame. extraction yields         horizons list instead carries $error         NULL pedon.

title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
text: every value originates photograph spatial prior, classification's evidence grade low construction (D VLM-extracted attributes, C SoilGrids prior contributed). result screening estimate, substitute described sampled profile.

title: Classify a soil profile from field photographs alone — classify_from_photos
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Live use with an ellmer chat: res <- classify_from_photos(   images   = list(profile = "profile.jpg", fieldsheet = "sheet.jpg"),   lat = -22.7, lon = -43.6, country = "BR",   provider = ellmer::chat_anthropic()) res$wrb$name res$wrb$evidence_grade   # "D" or "C" } # }

what: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
text: v0.7 ligou 13 ordens; v0.7.1 desce ao 2o nivel (subordens) via run_sibcs_subordem; v0.7.3 desce ao 3o nivel (Grandes Grupos) via run_sibcs_grande_grupo para ordens progressivamente wiradas em inst/rules/sibcs5/grandes-grupos/<ordem>.yaml (Cap 14 Organossolos primeiro). Quando subordem ainda nao tem bloco de Grandes Grupos, ou quando nenhum Grande Grupo passa (e nao ha catch-default), classificacao para 2o nivel.

title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
code: classify_sibcs(   pedon,   rules = NULL,   on_missing = c("warn", "silent", "error"),   include_familia = FALSE,   gapfill = FALSE )

title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
text: pedon PedonRecord. rules Conjunto de regras pre-carregado. on_missing Um de "warn" (default), "silent", "error". include_familia Quando TRUE (default FALSE), adiciona o 5o nivel categorico (Familia) via classify_sibcs_familia. O label textual da Familia aparece em $trace$familia_label, e lista de FamilyAttributes em $trace$familia. gapfill Preenchimento opcional de lacunas por interpolacao intra-perfil, default FALSE (-op; classificacao byte-identica). TRUE preenche celulas NA interiores dos atributos continuos por profundidade; um vetor de caracteres restringe aos atributos citados; uma lista nomeada e repassada gapfill_within_pedon. Celulas preenchidas recebem proveniencia inferred_prior, baixando o grau de evidencia para "C". Opera sobre copia profunda – o pedon chamador nunca e modificado.

title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
text: Um ClassificationResult cujo name eh o         nome completo da classe atribuida nivel mais profundo         (Grande Grupo > Subordem > Ordem) e rsg_or_order eh         o nome da ordem (e.g. "Organossolos"). Os codigos de cada         nivel e o trace ficam em $trace.

title: Classifica um pedon segundo o SiBCS 5a edicao (1o + 2o + 3o + 4o niveis) — classify_sibcs
code: pedon <- make_latossolo_canonical() res <- classify_sibcs(pedon) res$name #> [1] "Latossolos Vermelhos Distroficos tipicos"

what: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: Aplica dimensoes pertinentes ordem solo e devolve uma lista nomeada de FamilyAttribute. O label textual da Familia eh formado adicionando-se cada value nao-nulo apos designacao 4o nivel, separados por virgulas (Cap 18, p 281).

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
code: classify_sibcs_familia(   pedon,   ordem_code = NULL,   sg_code = NULL,   max_depth_cm = 200 )

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: pedon PedonRecord. ordem_code Codigo da ordem (1 letra: "P", "L", ...). Se NULL, sera derivado de sg_code. sg_code Codigo subgrupo 4o nivel (e.g. "PVdAr"). Opcional; usado para ajustes especificos por SG (e.g. forcar subgrupamento textural em arenicos/espessarenicos). max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200 cm).

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: Lista nomeada de FamilyAttribute.

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: Esta funcao NAO eh uma chave determinista: cada perfil recebe SIMULTANEAMENTE todos os adjetivos pertinentes (multi-rotulo).

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: Implementadas 5 dimensoes – grupamento textural, subgrupamento textural, distribuicao de cascalhos, constituicao esqueletica, tipo de horizonte superficial. Outras dimensoes (prefixos epi/ meso/endo, saturacao de bases, alico, mineralogia, atividade da argila, oxidos de ferro, andico, especificos de Organossolos) adicionadas em sub-commits subsequentes.

title: Classifica um perfil no 5o nivel categorico do SiBCS (Familia) — classify_sibcs_familia
text: Embrapa (2018), SiBCS 5a ed., Cap 18, pp 281-288.

what: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
text: Walks canonical USDA key (Order -> Suborder -> Great Group -> Subgroup) using YAML rule files : inst/rules/usda/key.yaml: Order key (12 entries) inst/rules/usda/suborders/<order>.yaml inst/rules/usda/great-groups/<order>.yaml inst/rules/usda/subgroups/<order>.yaml

title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
code: classify_usda(   pedon,   rules = NULL,   on_missing = c("warn", "silent", "error"),   include_family = FALSE,   infer_temperature = TRUE,   gapfill = FALSE )

title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
text: pedon PedonRecord. rules Optional pre-loaded rule set. on_missing One "warn" (default), "silent", "error". include_family TRUE, derive prepend 5th-level family modifiers. Default FALSE (output byte-identical earlier versions). infer_temperature deriving family, infer soil temperature regime latitude/elevation site$soil_temperature_regime absent (default TRUE). See family_temperature_regime_usda. gapfill Opt-within-pedon depth gap-fill, default FALSE (-op, classification stays byte-identical). TRUE fills interior NA cells continuous depth-trending attributes linear interpolation profile's measured horizons; character vector restricts attributes; named list passed gapfill_within_pedon. Filled cells carry inferred_prior provenance, evidence grade drops "C". Runs deep copy – caller's pedon never mutated.

title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
text: ClassificationResult deepest-level         taxon name. level's trace $trace; family         attributes $trace$family.

title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
text: include_family = TRUE additionally derives 5th category, family – set class modifiers (particle-size, mineralogy, CEC-activity, reaction, temperature regime, depth) PREPENDED subgroup name, e.g. "fine, kaolinitic, isohyperthermic Rhodic Hapludox". See classify_usda_family.

title: Classify a pedon under USDA Soil Taxonomy (13th edition) — classify_usda
text: Soil Survey Staff (2022). Keys Soil Taxonomy, 13th   edition. USDA Natural Resources Conservation Service.

code: pedon <- make_ferralsol_canonical() res <- classify_usda(pedon) res$name #> [1] "Rhodic Hapludox" # include the 5th (family) level: classify_usda(pedon, include_family = TRUE)$name #> [1] "fine, mixed, subactive, isothermic Rhodic Hapludox"

what: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
text: Sends soilKey PedonRecord SmartSolosExpert REST endpoint maintained Embrapa (Glauber Vaz's PROLOG-based implementation SiBCS classifier) returns resulting four-level classification (Ordem / Subordem / Grande Grupo / Subgrupo) wrapped soilKey ClassificationResult.

title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
code: classify_via_smartsolos_api(   pedon,   api_key = Sys.getenv("AGROAPI_TOKEN"),   endpoint = c("classification", "verification"),   drenagem = NULL,   reference_sibcs = NULL,   base_url = "https://api.cnptia.embrapa.br/smartsolos/expert/v1",   timeout_seconds = 30,   post_fn = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
text: pedon PedonRecord. api_key Bearer token. Defaults Sys.getenv("AGROAPI_TOKEN"). Required unless post_fn supplied (test injection). endpoint One "classification" (default; classify ) "verification" (classify + compare user-supplied reference_sibcs). drenagem Optional drainage class. Integer 1..8 Portuguese string ("bem drenado" etc.). reference_sibcs Optional named list (ordem, subordem, gde_grupo, subgrupo) used "verification" endpoint user's reference. base_url Override base URL. Default "https://api.cnptia.embrapa.br/smartsolos/expert/v1". timeout_seconds HTTP timeout (default 30). post_fn Internal: function signature function(payload) -> response_list unit tests. supplied, network bypassed. verbose TRUE (default), emits one-line summary.

title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
text: ClassificationResult         system = "SiBCS 5a edicao (SmartSolosExpert API)"         four taxonomic levels         rsg_or_order (Ordem) qualifiers         (Subordem / GdeGrupo / Subgrupo). Verification-mode         responses additionally carry trace$smartsolos_summary         (per-level match counters L0..L4).

title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
text: **external classifier** – package host replicate PROLOG rules. function exists soilKey users can cross-validate local classifier authoritative Embrapa-hosted reference. Use "verification" endpoint compare user-supplied reference classification (API returns per-level match summary counters L0..L4). Authentication: register free AgroAPI account https://www.agroapi.cnptia.embrapa.br/portal/, subscribe SmartSolosExpert API generate access token. Pass via AGROAPI_TOKEN environment variable api_key argument.

title: Classify a PedonRecord via Embrapa's SmartSolosExpert REST API — classify_via_smartsolos_api
text: Vaz, G. J., Silva Neto, L. de F. da, & Barbedo, J. G. . (2025). SmartSolos Expert: expert system Brazilian soil classification. Smart Agricultural Technology, 10, 100735. doi:10.1016/j.atech.2024.100735 . Vaz, G. J., Silva Neto, L. de F. da, Lima, R. N., & Oliveira, S. R. de M. (2019). Uma API para classificacao de solos Brasil. Anais 12 Congresso Brasileiro de Agroinformatica (SBIAGRO 2019), pp. 63-72. Ponta Grossa. Vaz, G. J., Silva Jr, . F., & Silva Neto, L. de F. da (2023). Brazilian soil data taxonomic classification. Redape, V1. doi:10.48432/PYKKA7 .

what: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
title: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
text: Runs n Monte-Carlo perturbations pedon tallies resulting classes empirical posterior. Unlike classification_robustness, perturbation magnitude every (horizon, attribute) cell scaled provenance evidence grade (see get_perturbation_scale): -grade measurement nudged percent, E-grade assumption third value. posterior therefore reflects just close profile sits key boundary, trustworthy inputs placed actually .

title: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
code: classify_with_uncertainty(   pedon,   n = 200L,   system = c("wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda"),   level = c("rsg", "name"),   scales = NULL,   sensitivity = TRUE,   seed = 42L )

title: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
text: pedon PedonRecord. n Number Monte-Carlo draws (default 200). system One "wrb2022", "sibcs", "usda". level "rsg" (default; compare RSG / order) "name" (compare full classification name, qualifiers included – strictly uncertain). scales Optional named list overriding default per-grade magnitudes; element shape returned get_perturbation_scale, keyed grade letter. sensitivity TRUE (default) also computes leave-one-attribute-sensitivity ranking. Set FALSE skip extra pass posterior needed. seed Random seed reproducibility.

title: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
text: list class "soilkey_uncertainty" elements:         posterior (named numeric vector summing 1, sorted         descending), top1 (modal class), entropy         (Shannon entropy posterior, natural log), sensitivity         (data.table attribute / importance,         NULL), n_runs, n_success,         baseline, system level.

title: Posterior distribution over classification outcomes — classify_with_uncertainty
code: # \donttest{ p <- make_ferralsol_canonical() u <- classify_with_uncertainty(p, n = 50, system = "wrb2022") u$posterior   # P(RSG = x) #> Ferralsols  #>          1  u$entropy     # near 0 for a robust profile #> [1] 0 # }

what: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022
title: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022
text: High-level classification entry point. Pre-computes implemented diagnostic horizons (argic, ferralic, mollic) transparent reporting, runs key, assembles ClassificationResult trace, ambiguities, missing-data hints, evidence grade, (future) prior sanity check.

title: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022
code: classify_wrb2022(   pedon,   prior = NULL,   prior_threshold = 0.01,   on_missing = c("warn", "silent", "error"),   rules = NULL,   strict = NULL,   specifiers = FALSE,   gapfill = FALSE )

title: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022
text: pedon PedonRecord. prior Optional spatial prior – data.table columns rsg_code probability, typically return value spatial_prior. supplied, result records prior_check entry prior_consistency_check; inconsistent prior also emits warning. deterministic key NEVER overridden prior. prior_threshold Probability prior triggers "inconsistent" warning (default 0.01). on_missing One "warn" (default), "silent", "error". Behaviour trace reports missing attributes. rules Optional pre-loaded rule set. strict Logical NULL. Controls WRB Tier-3 strict mode per-RSG numerical gates (Vertisols, Andosols, Gleysols, Planosols, Ferralsols, Chernozems, Kastanozems). NULL (default) gates follow getOption("soilKey.rsg_strict", FALSE). Passing TRUE FALSE forces strict mode duration call; see individual RSG-gate help pages (e.g. ferralsol) strengthened thresholds. specifiers Logical. TRUE, auto-attach WRB 2022 Ch 5 depth specifiers (Epi-/Endo-/Bathy-/Amphi-/Panto-/Kato-) depth-anchored qualifiers based diagnostic feature's actual depth – e.g. gleyic feature confined 50–100 cm yields Endogleyic instead Gleyic. Default FALSE keeps canonical names byte-identical. Surface / epipedon qualifiers excluded (depth definitional). gapfill Opt-within-pedon depth gap-fill, default FALSE (-op, classification stays byte-identical). TRUE fills interior NA cells continuous depth-trending attributes linear interpolation profile's measured horizons; character vector restricts attributes; named list passed gapfill_within_pedon. Filled cells carry inferred_prior provenance, evidence grade drops "C". Runs deep copy – caller's pedon never mutated.

title: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022
text: ClassificationResult.

title: Classify a pedon under WRB 2022 — classify_wrb2022

"usda_subgroup" canonical reference Soil Taxonomy 13th-edition subgroup set kst13_codes; "wrb_qualifiers" WRB 2022 principal + supplementary qualifier set wrb2022_canonical.

title: Honest taxonomic-completeness report — coverage_report
text: system axis measure. USDA taxon levels Soil Taxonomy 13th-edition code set (kst13_codes): "usda_subgroup" (default), "usda_great_group", "usda_suborder". WRB 2022 qualifiers wrb2022_canonical: "wrb_qualifiers" – "covered" means qual_* function exists genuine implementation (unconditional passed = NA stub), inert ones returned $stubs. "sibcs" external canonical class list, honestly reports registered class counts per level (percentage). write TRUE, also write Markdown summary report_dir. Default FALSE. report_dir Directory Markdown report write = TRUE. Defaults inst/benchmarks/reports inside installed package.

title: Honest taxonomic-completeness report — coverage_report
text: Invisibly, list $overall (one-row data frame:   system, level, canonical_n, registered_n,   covered_n, missing_n, pct), $by_group (per   order, per principal/supplementary), $missing (canonical names   registered), $extra (registered names absent canonical   set), – "wrb_qualifiers" – $stubs (functions   exist inert). compact summary printed side effect.

title: Cryic conditions (WRB 2022) — cryic_conditions
text: Tests whether continuous frozen / permafrost material occurs within upper max_top_cm. Two alternative paths qualify per WRB 2022: Permafrost temperature: layer top_cm <=         max_top_cm (default 100)         permafrost_temp_C <= max_temp_C (default 0 C). Designation pattern: layer top_cm <=         max_top_cm designation containing suffix         "f" (frozen) matching "^Cf" / "perma".         Used fallback temperature field         pedon (typical legacy survey data). Either path qualifies. Diagnostic Cryosols.

title: Cryic conditions (WRB 2022) — cryic_conditions
code: cryic_conditions(pedon, max_top_cm = 100, max_temp_C = 0)

title: Cryic conditions (WRB 2022) — cryic_conditions
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) (default 100). max_temp_C Maximum mean annual permafrost-zone temperature (deg C) temperature path (default 0).

title: Pre-2018 SiBCS Order names -> SiBCS 5a edicao plural Title Case map — .SIBCS_LEGACY_ORDER_MAP
text: BDsolos exports collected SiBCS 5a edicao (2018) carry historical Order names modern classifier emit. common cases observed RJ.csv (722 perfis): Podzolicos (54 perfis em RJ) -> Argissolos         (post-2018 Order Argissolos absorveu o Podzolico Vermelho-         Amarelo, Podzolico Vermelho-Escuro, etc.) Gleis      (44 perfis em RJ) -> Gleissolos         (Gleis Humico, Gleis Pouco Humico colapsaram em Gleissolos) Aluviais   (13 perfis em RJ) -> Neossolos         (Solos Aluviais foram reclassificados para Neossolos         Fluvicos SiBCS 5a edicao, mas normalisacao aqui         emite apenas Ordem moderna Neossolos –         Subordem Neossolos Fluvicos nao eh recuperavel         label legado antigo ALUVIAIS (granularidade         de Subordem se perde). Para benchmark Order-level isso e         suficiente; para Subordem o legado nao se mapeia.) Solos      -> NA         ("Solos Halomorficos", "Solos Hidromorficos", e fragmentos         de label UI antigo BDsolos onde Ordem nao foi         registrada). NA aqui significa "fora de scope para         comparacao". Aplicado em normalise_febr_sibcs(level = "order") apos pluralisacao normal. Para subordem o legacy mapping ainda nao e aplicado (ver TODO v0.9.61: estender para Subordem com "Podzolico Vermelho-Amarelo" -> "Argissolos Vermelho-Amarelos").

what: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
text: Drives Embrapa BDsolos web form via headless Chrome (chromote) produce CSV profiles + attributes. Marked **experimental**: heavy queries (UF filter) frequently overload Embrapa server. Prefer filter_uf = batches one two states time stitch resulting CSVs.

title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
code: download_bdsolos(   out_path,   accept_terms = FALSE,   filter_uf = NULL,   attributes = "default",   timeout_seconds = 600,   chromote_session = NULL,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
text: out_path File path downloaded CSV. accept_terms Logical. Must TRUE proceed; function aborts otherwise. Documents informed consent BDsolos terms (personal/academic use, ABNT citation). filter_uf Optional 2-letter UF code (e.g. "RJ", "SC"). Strongly recommended – full-table query often times . attributes Character vector. attribute groups request. Defaults full SiBCS-classification-relevant set (Identificacao + Localizacao + Classificacao Pontos de Amostragem, Identificacao + Morfologicas + Fisicas + Quimicas Horizontes; Mineralogicas excluded performance). Pass "" include Mineralogicas. timeout_seconds Total timeout AJAX query. Default 600 (10 min). chromote_session Optional pre-built chromote::ChromoteSession. Useful share session across calls. verbose TRUE (default), prints progress.

title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos

title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
text: Per Embrapa terms--use, data licensed personal / academic use publications must cite source per ABNT. Set accept_terms = TRUE acknowledge let function click "Concordo" behalf.

title: Download the BDsolos consulta-publica CSV (experimental, requires chromote) — download_bdsolos
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Single UF (fast, recommended) download_bdsolos("soil_data/bdsolos/RJ.csv",                   accept_terms = TRUE,                   filter_uf    = "RJ")  # Stitch multiple UFs for (uf in c("RJ", "SP", "MG", "ES")) {   download_bdsolos(file.path("soil_data/bdsolos",                                paste0(uf, ".csv")),                     accept_terms = TRUE, filter_uf = uf) }  # Then load all of them csvs <- list.files("soil_data/bdsolos", "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE) all_pedons <- unlist(lapply(csvs, load_bdsolos_csv), recursive = FALSE) length(all_pedons) } # }

text: Fetches region-filtered subset Open Soil Spectral Library (Sanderman et al. 2024) assembles `list(Xr, Yr, metadata)` shape consumed predict_ossl_mbl predict_ossl_plsr_local. result cached `tools::R_user_dir("soilKey", "cache")` subsequent calls session (future R sessions) skip network.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
code: download_ossl_subset(   region = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia",     "oceania"),   properties = c("clay_pct", "sand_pct", "silt_pct", "cec_cmol", "bs_pct", "ph_h2o",     "oc_pct", "fe_dcb_pct", "caco3_pct"),   wavelengths = 350:2500,   endpoint = NULL,   cache_dir = NULL,   force = FALSE,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: region One "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia", "oceania". Filters OSSL training rows site coordinates' continent. properties Character vector OSSL property names keep `Yr` (drops reference columns keep artefact small). Defaults WRB-relevant set used fill_from_spectra. wavelengths Integer vector wavelengths (nm) returned Xr matrix interpolated . Defaults Vis-NIR/SWIR (350-2500 nm 1-nm resolution, 2151 columns). endpoint OSSL HTTP endpoint serving JSON manifest; overrideable via options(soilKey.ossl_endpoint = ...) testing using private mirror. default public Soil Spectroscopy GG bucket. cache_dir Cache directory; defaults tools::R_user_dir("soilKey", "cache"). force TRUE, re-fetches even cached subset exists. verbose TRUE, emits `cli` summary fetch.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: list elements Xr (numeric matrix, rows =         training profiles, columns = wavelengths nm),         Yr (data.frame requested property columns,         rows aligned Xr), metadata (snapshot         date, region, n profiles, source URL, SHA-256         cache file). Pass ossl_library         argument fill_from_spectra         predict_ossl_mbl.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: function intentionally fall back synthetic predictor network failure – missing OSSL artefact real condition caller must handle, silent fallback make benchmarks meaningless.

title: Download an OSSL subset and return an `ossl_library` artefact — download_ossl_subset
text: Sanderman, J., Savage, K., Dangal, S.R.S., Duran, G., Rivard, C., Cardona, M.T., Sandzhieva, ., Aramian, . & Safanelli, J.L. (2024). Soil Spectroscopy Global Good – Open Soil Spectral Library (OSSL). https://soilspectroscopy.org/.

what: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: Fetches region-filtered slice Open Soil Spectral Library via download_ossl_subset post-joins WRB Reference Soil Group labels WoSIS GraphQL spatial nearest-neighbour. resulting artefact canonical list(Xr, Yr, metadata) shape – extra columns Yr: wrb_rsg, wrb_label_source, wrb_label_distance_km, plus optionally sibcs_ordem usda_order translate_systems = TRUE.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
code: download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region = c("global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia",     "oceania"),   max_distance_km = 5,   wosis_endpoint = NULL,   translate_systems = TRUE,   max_to_label = Inf,   verbose = TRUE,   query_fn = NULL,   ... )

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: region OSSL region filter; one "global", "south_america", "north_america", "europe", "africa", "asia", "oceania". max_distance_km WoSIS spatial-join tolerance kilometres (default 5). Profiles whose nearest WRB-labeled WoSIS neighbour farther left unlabeled. wosis_endpoint Override WoSIS GraphQL endpoint (default getOption("soilKey.wosis_graphql")). canonical value "https://graphql.isric.org/wosis/graphql". translate_systems TRUE (default), also adds sibcs_ordem usda_order columns derived WRB label via Schad (2023) Annex Table 1 / SiBCS 5ª ed. Annex correspondence. translations 1:N classes; pick -common partner tag rows translation genuinely ambiguous. max_to_label Maximum number profiles query WoSIS (default Inf). WoSIS throttles aggressive queries; cap running interactive demos. verbose Emit cli progress messages. query_fn Optional injection per-coordinate WoSIS query function. Default uses .query_nearest_wosis_wrb. Tests pass stub exercise join logic without network. ... Forwarded download_ossl_subset.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: list Xr (numeric matrix), Yr (data         frame labels attached), metadata         (list OSSL fetch metadata + join statistics:         number profiles labeled, average / max distance,         WoSIS endpoint, snapshot date).

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: OSSL stores Vis-NIR / MIR spectra lab data typically lacks WRB Reference Soil Group labels profiles (KSSL data USDA-flavoured; non-US contributions inconsistent). WoSIS, contrast, archives ~228 000 profiles WRB labels spectra. function bridges two user can run classify_by_spectral_neighbours real-data OSSL library without spatial join .

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
text: WRB labels obtained via spatial join weak labels. physical location may classified differently across surveys (different WRB editions, different interpretations). row carries: wrb_label_source = "wosis_spatial_join": label         inherited WoSIS neighbour within         max_distance_km. wrb_label_distance_km: distance         neighbour (NA neighbour found within         tolerance). wrb_label_source = "ossl_native": label         already present OSSL Yr (rare; preserved verbatim). wrb_label_source = "missing": neighbour within         tolerance; row stays unlabeled skipped         downstream. Treat labels priors, ground truth.

title: Download an OSSL subset and attach WRB / SiBCS / USDA labels — download_ossl_subset_with_labels
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Real OSSL South-America subset with WRB labels: lib <- download_ossl_subset_with_labels(   region          = "south_america",   max_distance_km = 10 ) table(lib$Yr$wrb_rsg, useNA = "always") table(lib$Yr$wrb_label_source)  # Drop into the spectral analogy classifier: res <- classify_by_spectral_neighbours(   spectrum     = my_query_spectrum,   ossl_library = lib,   k            = 25,   region       = list(lat = -22.7, lon = -43.7,                       radius_km = 500) ) } # }

what: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: Classifica mineralogia da argila para Argissolos, Cambissolos, Plintossolos, Luvissolos, Nitossolos, Vertissolos, Chernossolos, Planossolos, Gleissolos quando ha informacao quantitativa de atividade da argila e/ou Ki/Kr. Cobre classes nao endereçadas por familia_mineralogia_argila_latossolo: esmectitica: T_argila >= ta_threshold (default         27 cmolc/kg argila), indicando dominancia de argilas 2:1         expansivas (esmectita / vermiculita / micas hidratadas). caulinitica: Ki >= ki_caulinitico_min (default         0.75) e Kr >= kr_caulinitico_min (default 0.75),         alem de T_argila < ta_threshold. oxidica: Kr < kr_caulinitico_min, indicando         predominancia de oxihidrooxidos de Fe e Al. mista: nenhum dos outros gates fechou         conclusivamente – evidencia heterogenea ou incompleta. Quando os tres atributos (T_argila, Ki, Kr) estiverem ausentes, o resultado fica NULL e os atributos faltantes sao reportados.

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
code: familia_mineralogia_argila_geral(   pedon,   max_depth_cm = 200,   ta_threshold = 27,   ki_caulinitico_min = 0.75,   kr_caulinitico_min = 0.75 )

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth_cm Profundidade da secao de controle (default 200). ta_threshold Limite cmolc/kg argila para esmectitica (default 27). ki_caulinitico_min Limite Ki para caulinitica (default 0.75). kr_caulinitico_min Limite Kr para caulinitica vs oxidica (default 0.75).

title: Familia: mineralogia da fracao argila (geral, nao-Latossolos) — familia_mineralogia_argila_geral

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: Tests whether horizon meets ferralic horizon criteria. ferralic horizon subsurface horizon resulting long intense weathering, characterized low cation exchange capacity per unit clay – canonical "low-activity clay" signal defines Ferralsol RSG.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
code: ferralic(pedon, min_thickness = 30, max_cec = NULL, engine = NULL)

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness cm (default 30). max_cec Maximum CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) per kg clay (default NULL = 16 soilkey engine, 20 aqp engine; see engine). engine One "soilkey" (default; strict 16 cmol_c/kg-clay threshold per WRB 2022) "aqp" (relaxed 20 cmol_c/kg-clay – regional tolerance accommodates Brazilian / SOTERLAC Latossolos data, Embrapa-style Mehlich/Ca+Mg+K+Al sum often reads ~17-20 profiles canonical NRCS / WRB definition accept ferralic). NULL reads getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine"). numeric threshold can also overridden directly via options(soilKey.ferralic_max_cec = ...).

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: Sub-tests called: test_ferralic_texture – texture sandy loam         finer. test_cec_per_clay – CEC / clay <= 16 (20         engine = "aqp") cmol_c/kg clay. test_ferralic_thickness – thickness >= 30 cm. v0.3.1 alignment WRB 2022 Ch 3.1.10 (p. 44): older "ECEC <= 12 cmol_c/kg clay" gate removed canonical text – CEC (1M NH4OAc, pH 7) <= 16 required. v0.9.67 regional tolerance: BDsolos RJ benchmark (n=722 perfis) showed 88/115 Latossolos failing strict 16-cmol gate Embrapa lab methodology often reads CEC 17-20 profiles unambiguously Latossolos every criterion. engine = "aqp" threshold 20 closes gap without redefining WRB threshold ; users targeting strict WRB 2022 fidelity keep engine = "soilkey". weatherable-mineral test (<= 10% volume), water-dispersible-clay test, stratification / rock-structure exclusions remain deferred (need mineralogical data outside canonical horizon schema) refinements rather gates.

title: Ferralic horizon (WRB 2022) — ferralic
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022). World Reference Base Soil Resources, 4th edition. International Union Soil Sciences, Vienna. Chapter 3.1.10 – Ferralic horizon (p. 44).

what: Ferralsol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 110) — ferralsol
title: Ferralsol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 110) — ferralsol
text: WRB-canonical: ferralic horizon \<= 150 cm argic horizon starting (upper limit ) ferralic, UNLESS argic upper 30 cm throughout one : < 10% water-dispersible clay; DeltapH (pH_KCl - pH_water) \>= 0; \>= 1.4% soil organic carbon. v0.3.4 enforces three exception paths. DeltapH check uses ph_kcl ph_h2o; WDC check uses water_dispersible_clay_pct (introduced v0.3.3 schema).

Used subset OSSL training data supported underlying backend. properties Character vector OSSL-supported property names predict. Default covers -requested WRB/SiBCS-relevant attributes. method One "mbl", "plsr_local", "pretrained". preprocess Pre-processing pipeline; passed preprocess_spectra. k_neighbors Number neighbours memory-based methods. overwrite FALSE (default), fill cells whose existing provenance weaker predicted_spectra. ossl_library Optional OSSL library object (see predict_ossl_mbl). ossl_models Optional named list pretrained models (see predict_ossl_pretrained). verbose TRUE, prints cli summary.

what: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
text: requested attribute, builds depth profile horizons attribute measured (non-NA) linearly interpolates value mid-depth every horizon missing – horizons whose mid-depth falls strictly shallowest deepest measured layer. Cells top bottom measured layer left NA: function interpolates, never extrapolates. fill written source = "inferred_prior", PedonRecord authority order keeps displacing measured, spectra-predicted VLM-extracted value, downstream compute_evidence_grade call reports grade "C".

title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
code: gapfill_within_pedon(pedon, attrs = NULL, confidence = 0.6, overwrite = FALSE)

title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
text: pedon PedonRecord least two horizons. attrs Character vector horizon columns fill. Defaults continuous depth-trending attributes linear interpolation can reasonably estimate (clay/silt/sand, pH, organic carbon, CEC/ECEC, base/aluminium saturation, bulk density). confidence Numeric \[0, 1\] recorded provenance confidence interpolated cell. Defaults 0.6 – measured value anchored profile's data, hence 0.5 used external SoilGrids prior. overwrite FALSE (default) NA cells filled. TRUE, non-measured cells re-interpolated (measured cells still never overwritten, provenance authority order always respected).

title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
text: Invisibly, mutated pedon. attribute         "gapfill_within_pedon" return value records many         cells filled attributes.

title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
text: within-pedon companion apply_soilgrids_depth_prior (fills external SoilGrids profile rather profile's measured layers). mechanism behind opt-gapfill argument classify_wrb2022, classify_sibcs, classify_usda classify_all. Note mutates pedon place (apply_soilgrids_depth_prior ). gapfill argument classifiers operates deep copy instead, classification call never alters caller's pedon.

title: Fill interior missing horizon attributes by within-pedon depth interpolation — gapfill_within_pedon
code: h <- data.frame(   top_cm    = c(0, 20, 40, 60),   bottom_cm = c(20, 40, 60, 90),   clay_pct  = c(15, NA, 35, 40) ) p <- PedonRecord$new(horizons = h) gapfill_within_pedon(p, attrs = "clay_pct") p$horizons$clay_pct   # second horizon filled to ~25 by interpolation #> [1] 15 25 35 40

title: Gleyic properties (WRB 2022) — gleyic_properties
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) candidate layer (default 50, per WRB 2022). min_redox_pct Minimum redoximorphic_features_pct (default 5). stagnic_decay_factor Numeric threshold option (see Details).

title: Gleyic properties (WRB 2022) — gleyic_properties
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Gleyic properties (WRB 2022) — gleyic_properties
text: Sub-test: test_gleyic_features – requires explicit redoximorphic_features_pct >= 5% within upper 50 cm. v0.2 deliberately use Munsell-based shortcut (chroma <= 2 + value >= 4) primary criterion: pattern fits albic / bleached horizons Podzols just well truly reduced gleyic horizons. v0.3 add reductimorphic / oxidimorphic feature discrimination model field-described mottle properties. v0.9.72 adds designation-suffix path (opt-).

title: Gleyic properties (WRB 2022) — gleyic_properties
text: Field-described Brazilian Gleissolos profiles (e.g.\ Embrapa Redape curated dataset) routinely encode gleyic properties via designation suffix g (e.g.\ Cg, Cg1, Cgn, Apg) plus low-chroma Munsell colours (chroma \<= 2), without recording redoximorphic_features_pct numeric percent. strict canonical test returns NA every horizon Gleissolos cascade Orders. options(soilKey.gleyic_designation_inference = TRUE) function accepts layer gleyic : canonical redoximorphic_features_pct test         NA layer, designation matches [-Z]+g[0-9a-z]? (horizon         name g suffix master letter sequence,         e.g.\ Cg, Bg2, Apg, Cgn), layer munsell_chroma_moist <= 2 (low-chroma         reduced colour) Munsell recorded; Munsell         missing layer suffix alone sufficient         (designation suffix direct signal pedologist         field judgment). conservative: suffix g master-letter modifier FAO/Embrapa horizon nomenclature explicitly means "gleyic-affected" – curator already made call. Default FALSE (canonical behaviour preserved).

title: Gleyic properties (WRB 2022) — gleyic_properties
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 3, Gleyic properties.

what: Gleysol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 103) — gleysol
title: Gleysol RSG gate (WRB 2022 Ch 4, p 103) — gleysol
text: WRB-canonical (multi-path): Layer \>= 25 cm starting \<= 40 cm gleyic properties         throughout reducing conditions parts every         sublayer; Mollic/umbric > 40 cm thick reducing conditions         parts every sublayer 40 cm mineral surface lower         limit, directly underneath layer \>= 10 cm lower         limit \>= 65 cm gleyic properties + reducing         conditions; Permanent saturation water \<= 40 cm. v0.3.4 enforces path 1 (dominant path) path 3 via designation (W / saturated marker). Path 2 deferred (requires depth-- saturation column standard).

what: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: Runs mpspline2::mpspline_tidy() requested numeric horizon attribute, producing new PedonRecord per input pedon whose horizons table covers canonical GSM intervals (GSM_DEPTHS). Categorical attributes (designation, Munsell hue) propagated mode--depth-overlap.

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
code: harmonize_to_gsm(   pedons,   attributes = c("clay_pct", "silt_pct", "sand_pct", "ph_h2o", "oc_pct", "cec_cmol",     "base_saturation_pct", "munsell_value_moist", "munsell_chroma_moist",     "redoximorphic_features_pct"),   depths = GSM_DEPTHS,   lam = 0.1,   verbose = TRUE )

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: pedons list PedonRecord objects. attributes Character vector numeric horizon column names harmonise. Default covers chemistry / texture / Munsell numeric columns soilKey diagnostics use. depths Numeric vector GSM depth boundaries (n+1 values n intervals). Default GSM_DEPTHS. lam Smoothing parameter spline (default 0.1, per Bishop et al. 1999 recommendation). verbose TRUE (default), emits cli progress.

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: list new PedonRecord objects   harmonised horizons.

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: Bishop et al. (1999) spline conserves integral attribute depth: original pedon 30 g/kg OC 0-15 cm, harmonised pedon report 30 g/kg integrated 0-15 cm (split 0-5 5-15 proportion spline-implied gradient). critical property benchmark integrity: simple linear interpolation preserve mass biases means upward / downward systematically.

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: designation munsell_hue_moist (character columns horizon schema) splined. Instead, target GSM interval, pick modal value weighted depth-overlap fraction input horizons. Ties broken uppermost-input-horizon precedence.

title: Harmonise pedons to GlobalSoilMap depth intervals — harmonize_to_gsm
text: Bishop, T.F.., McBratney, .B., Laslett, G.M. (1999). "Modelling soil attribute depth functions equal-area quadratic smoothing splines." Geoderma 91: 27-45. Arrouays, D. et al. (2014). "GlobalSoilMap: Toward fine-resolution global grid soil properties." Advances Agronomy 125: 93-134.

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
text: surface (near-surface, drainage) horizon organic material; diagnostic Histosols. Two alternative qualifying paths per WRB 2022: Contiguous: single layer organic material         (OC % >= min_oc) reaching surface         least min_thickness cm thick (default 10 cm). Cumulative: organic material totalling         cumulative_min_cm cm (default 40) within upper         cumulative_max_depth_cm (default 80). Relevant         folic / mossy Histosols slopes. Either path qualifies. "drainage" qualifier (recently drained organic soils) treated implicit since OC thickness criteria apply.

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
code: histic_horizon(   pedon,   min_thickness = 10,   min_oc = 12,   surface_top_cm = 0,   cumulative_min_cm = 40,   cumulative_max_depth_cm = 80 )

title: Histic horizon (WRB 2022) — histic_horizon
text: pedon PedonRecord. min_thickness Minimum thickness (cm) contiguous path (default 10). min_oc Minimum organic carbon % (default 12, WRB 2022; equivalent >= 20% organic matter). surface_top_cm Maximum top depth (cm) layer considered "surface-related" contiguous path (default 0). cumulative_min_cm Minimum cumulative thickness (cm) cumulative path (default 40). cumulative_max_depth_cm Depth window (cm) cumulative path (default 80).

what: Hyposulfidic material (WRB 2022 Ch 3.3.9): same inorganic sulfidic S and field pH as hypersulfidic but does NOT consist of hypersulfidic (criterion 3 – does not acidify to pH < 4 on aerobic incubation, usually self-neutralised by carbonate). Reachable from v0.9.128: when incubation_ph is measured, a sulfidic + pH>=4 layer that stays >= 4 on incubation is the set-complement of hypersulfidic_material and is reported here. Without an incubation pH the two cannot be told apart, so this returns empty (the layer is reported as potential hypersulfidic instead). — hyposulfidic_material

what: Flatten a classification key trace into a tabular form — key_trace_table
title: Flatten a classification key trace into a tabular form — key_trace_table
text: Normalises system-dependent decision trace carried ClassificationResult single, ordered data frame display rows. WRB 2022 stores flat list reference-soil-group test steps; hierarchical SiBCS USDA keys store nested list phases (orders, suborders, great groups, subgroups, family, ...), holding candidate steps, assigned-taxon record, family attributes, bare label. function walks shapes returns one row per step, order key evaluated, every consumer (print(), HTML PDF reports, Shiny app) can render trace uniformly.

title: Flatten a classification key trace into a tabular form — key_trace_table
code: key_trace_table(x)

title: Flatten a classification key trace into a tabular form — key_trace_table

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
text: Tests whether continuous rock rock-like material occurs within max_depth cm surface. Two alternative paths qualify per WRB 2022: Designation: layer depth <= max_depth         designation matching "^R" "^Cr"         (continuous rock weathered rock-like substrate). Coarse fragments: layer depth <= max_depth         coarse_fragments_pct >= min_coarse_pct (default         90% volume), interpreted rock-dominated even         R / Cr-designated. Either path qualifies.

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
code: leptic_features(pedon, max_depth = 25, min_coarse_pct = NULL, engine = NULL)

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
text: pedon PedonRecord. max_depth Maximum depth (cm) continuous rock rock-dominated material must appear (default 25). min_coarse_pct Minimum coarse-fragment percent coarse-fragments path (default 90 soilkey engine, 50 aqp engine; NULL picks default per engine). engine One "soilkey" (default; strict 90\ cfvo threshold) "aqp" (LUCAS-friendly relaxed 50\ requiring positive evidence rock contact – v0.9.66 tightening). thin-topsoil path fires horizon ending within max_depth also satisfies least one : () designation contains "R" (e.g.\ AR, BR, Cr, R, Rk), (b) coarse_fragments_pct >= 30 (gravelly), (c) deeper horizon R/Cr-designated. Users strong external prior (e.g.\ parent-material survey documents rock < 25 cm record horizon table) can opt back original v0.9.65 loose behaviour options(soilKey.leptic_assume_rock_below = TRUE). NULL (default) reads getOption("soilKey.diagnostic_engine").

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
text: DiagnosticResult.

title: Leptic features (WRB 2022) — leptic_features
text: IUSS Working Group WRB (2022), Chapter 5, Leptosols.

title: Load the bundled KSSL + NASIS morphological-enriched sample (v0.9.75) — load_kssl_nasis_sample
text: Compared load_kssl_sample (KSSL lab tables ), sample carries morphological evidence several WRB diagnostic horizons need: | Field | KSSL-| KSSL + NASIS | |——-|———-:|————-:| | munsell_hue_moist     | 0 | munsell_value_moist   | 0 | munsell_chroma_moist  | 0 | munsell_hue_dry       | 0 | structure_grade       | 0 | structure_type        | 0 | clay_films_amount     | 0 | slickensides          | 0 First-ever benchmark enriched sample (soilKey v0.9.75, full v0.9.69-72 fallback stack): Top-1 baseline: 19.1\         **+3.5pp lift purely NASIS morphology**) Top-1 full stack: 20.6\ Phaeozem: 1/33 -> 2/33 (Munsell-driven mollic detection) Podzol:   0/15 -> 1/15 Remaining ceiling driven attributes neither dataset preserves: Solonetz needs Na/ESP, Vertisols need slickensides + cracks (NASIS records 1.7 subsoil samples NASIS often lacks.
